backend-core.summary: This subsystem is the runtime backbone of stockAgents: a single FastAPI app (backend/app/main.py `create_app()`) whose lifespan constructs and wires the whole object graph into `app.state` — Settings (pydantic-settings, env prefix SA_), Database (one shared thread-locked SQLite connection in WAL mode), EventBus (persist every event to activity_log + fan out to asyncio.Queue subscribers), DataLoader, a Broker chosen by `trading_mode` (PaperBroker vs lazily-imported TossBroker), the Orchestrator (cycle/goal state machine), and WsManager. On startup it marks any cycles left 'running' by a dead process as 'aborted'. A background `_auto_cycle_loop` task re-reads `settings.auto_cycle_minutes` every iteration (0=off, poll every 30s) and periodically starts 'research' cycles, optionally chaining a 'trade' cycle when `auto_trade_after_research` is set; all start failures are logged and retried, never fatal to the loop. The `/ws` WebSocket sends a `snapshot` message first (agents/cycle/meeting from `Orchestrator.snapshot()`), then relays every EventBus payload verbatim; REST lives under `/api` and the built frontend (frontend/dist) is mounted at `/`. Shutdown cancels the auto task, stops goal+cycle, then closes the DB.

key_contracts.role: App assembly, lifespan wiring, auto-cycle background loop, WS endpoint, static frontend mount
key_contracts.api: create_app(settings: Settings | None = None, meeting_seconds: float = 4.0) -> FastAPI; module-level `app = create_app()`. Constants: FRONTEND_DIST = <repo>/frontend/dist; AUTO_CYCLE_POLL_SECONDS = 30.0. Lifespan (asynccontextmanager) order: s = settings or get_settings(); db = Database(s.db_path); db.execute("UPDATE cycles SET status='aborted', finished_at=datetime('now') WHERE status='running'"); bus = EventBus(db); loader = DataLoader(db); broker = TossBroker(s) if s.trading_mode=='live' (lazy import so paper mode never imports live deps) else PaperBroker(db, loader, s); orchestrator = Orchestrator(db, bus, s, loader=loader, broker=broker, meeting_seconds=meeting_seconds); sets app.state.{settings,db,bus,loader,broker,orchestrator,ws_manager=WsManager()}; auto_task = asyncio.create_task(_auto_cycle_loop(orchestrator, s)). Teardown: auto_task.cancel(); suppress(asyncio.CancelledError, Exception): await auto_task; try: await orchestrator.stop_goal(); await orchestrator.stop_cycle(); finally: db.close(). async _auto_cycle_loop(orchestrator, settings) -> None: while True: minutes = settings.auto_cycle_minutes; if minutes<=0: sleep(30); continue; sleep(minutes*60); re-check setting still >0 else continue; try orchestrator.start_cycle('research') except CycleInProgressError: continue; except Exception: log+continue; if settings.auto_trade_after_research: await orchestrator.cycle_task (suppress Exception) then await _run_trade_cycle(orchestrator). async _run_trade_cycle(orchestrator): loop start_cycle('trade'), on CycleInProgressError sleep 30 and retry, then await orchestrator.cycle_task suppressing exceptions. CORS: allow_origins localhost:5173/127.0.0.1:5173, credentials+all methods/headers. @app.websocket('/ws') handler: await state.ws_manager.handle(websocket, state.bus, state.orchestrator.snapshot()). If FRONTEND_DIST.is_dir(): app.mount('/', StaticFiles(directory=..., html=True), name='frontend'). FastAPI(title='Stock Agents Office', lifespan=lifespan). Orchestrator surface used here: start_cycle(kind: str='research') -> int (raises CycleInProgressError); cycle_task: asyncio.Task | None; stop_cycle() -> None; stop_goal() -> None; snapshot() -> dict.

key_contracts.role: Settings singleton — pydantic-settings with env prefix and .env file
key_contracts.api: class Settings(BaseSettings): model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix='SA_', env_file='.env', env_file_encoding='utf-8', extra='ignore'). Fields (name: type = default): trading_mode: Literal['paper','live']='paper'; universe: list[str] = [10 KRX ticker codes: '005930','000660','035420','005380','051910','006400','035720','068270','105560','012330']; initial_cash: int=10_000_000; commission: float=0.00015; sell_tax: float=0.0018; slippage: float=0.0005; max_mdd: float=0.30; min_trades: int=10; rr_ratio: float=3.0 (take-profit width = rr_ratio x stop width; TP always derived from stop); min_trades_per_year: float=12.0 (activity filter); rank_w_sharpe=0.35, rank_w_win_rate=0.15, rank_w_mdd=0.2, rank_w_cagr=0.3 (percent-rank weighted leaderboard score); auto_cycle_minutes: int=0 (0=off); auto_trade_after_research: bool=False; candidates_per_cycle: int=60; goal_win_rate: float=0.65; goal_validate_every: int=5; goal_max_cycles: int=200; db_path: str='stockagents.db'; toss_client_id: str=''; toss_client_secret: str=''; live_max_order_krw: int=100_000; live_daily_order_limit: int=5. @lru_cache def get_settings() -> Settings. Runtime config mutation works because loops re-read attributes off the shared Settings instance each iteration (PUT /api/config mutates it in place).

key_contracts.role: Thread-safe SQLite wrapper: WAL, single shared connection + threading.Lock, dict rows, additive migrations
key_contracts.api: class Database: __init__(self, path: str) — sqlite3.connect(path, check_same_thread=False); conn.row_factory = _dict_factory (dict per row via cursor.description); PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL; init_schema(). execute(sql, params: tuple|list = ()) -> list[dict]: under lock; SELECTs (cur.description not None) return fetchall(); writes commit and return [{'id': cur.lastrowid}]. executemany(sql, seq_of_params) -> None (commits). close() -> None. init_schema(): executescript(_SCHEMA of CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) then _add_columns(table, ((col, decl), ...)) additive ALTER TABLE migrations guarded by PRAGMA table_info — used to add paper_orders.decision_date TEXT / decision_price REAL, cycles.kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'research', reports.kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'research'. TABLES: ohlcv_cache(ticker TEXT, date TEXT, open/high/low/close/volume REAL NOT NULL, PK(ticker,date)); cycles(id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, started_at TEXT, finished_at TEXT, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'running' [running|done|aborted], params_json TEXT DEFAULT '{}', summary_json TEXT DEFAULT '{}', kind TEXT DEFAULT 'research' [research|validate|trade]); strategies(id PK, cycle_id INTEGER, template TEXT NOT NULL, params_json TEXT NOT NULL, universe_json TEXT DEFAULT '[]', status TEXT DEFAULT 'candidate'); backtests(id PK, strategy_id INTEGER NOT NULL, ticker TEXT NOT NULL, metrics_json TEXT NOT NULL, equity_curve_json TEXT DEFAULT '[]', created_at TEXT DEFAULT datetime('now')); trades(id PK, backtest_id INTEGER NOT NULL, entry_date, exit_date TEXT, entry_price, exit_price, net_ret, holding_days REAL); reports(id PK, cycle_id INTEGER, markdown TEXT NOT NULL, created_at, kind DEFAULT 'research' [research|validation]); activity_log(id PK, ts TEXT DEFAULT datetime('now'), agent TEXT, level TEXT DEFAULT 'info', event_type TEXT NOT NULL, message TEXT NOT NULL, data_json TEXT DEFAULT '{}'); paper_orders(id PK, ts, ticker TEXT, side TEXT, qty INTEGER, filled_price REAL, status TEXT, reason TEXT DEFAULT '', decision_date TEXT, decision_price REAL); paper_positions(ticker TEXT PK, qty INTEGER, avg_price REAL); portfolio_snapshots(id PK, ts, cash REAL, positions_value REAL, total_value REAL); paper_state(key TEXT PK, value TEXT); champion_history(id PK, strategy_id INTEGER NOT NULL, crowned_at TEXT DEFAULT datetime('now'), demoted_at TEXT).

key_contracts.role: EventBus: every publish = one activity_log INSERT + fan-out to all WS subscriber queues
key_contracts.api: @dataclass class Event: type: str; agent: str | None = None; level: str = 'info'; message: str = ''; data: dict = field(default_factory=dict). class EventBus: __init__(db: Database); subscribers: list[asyncio.Queue]. async publish(event: Event) -> None: ts = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(timespec='seconds'); INSERT INTO activity_log(ts, agent, level, event_type, message, data_json) with data_json = json.dumps(event.data, ensure_ascii=False, default=str); row_id from returned lastrowid. Payload shape: if event.type=='log' -> {'type':'log','id':row_id,'ts':ts,'agent':...,'level':...,'event_type':'log','message':...,'data':event.data} (mirrors an activity_log row); else -> {'type': event.type, **event.data} (event types: agent_state, meeting_start {meeting_id, agents:[a,b], topic}, meeting_end {meeting_id}, cycle_progress, leaderboard_update, ...). Then for q in list(self.subscribers): q.put_nowait(payload) — unbounded queues, non-blocking, no backpressure. subscribe() -> asyncio.Queue (appends new queue); unsubscribe(q) -> None (safe if absent).

key_contracts.role: WS connection manager: snapshot-first protocol, then relay bus payloads until disconnect
key_contracts.api: class WsManager: active: set[WebSocket]. async handle(websocket: WebSocket, bus: EventBus, snapshot: dict) -> None: accept(); add to active; queue = bus.subscribe(); send_json({'type':'snapshot', **snapshot}) FIRST; then run two tasks concurrently — _forward(queue, websocket): loop queue.get() -> websocket.send_json(payload); _drain(websocket): loop websocket.receive_text() so client close is detected promptly — via asyncio.wait(FIRST_COMPLETED); cancel+await the pending one (suppress CancelledError); re-raise any done-task exception that is not WebSocketDisconnect; finally bus.unsubscribe(queue) and active.discard(websocket). Snapshot payload (Orchestrator.snapshot()): {'agents': [{'id','state','detail'}...], 'cycle': <status()['cycle']>, 'meeting': {'id': hex8, 'agents': [a, b]} | None}. All server->client messages are JSON objects discriminated by 'type'; client sends nothing meaningful (frames only drained).

key_contracts.role: REST surface (read for endpoint shapes; APIRouter(prefix='/api'))
key_contracts.api: GET /api/status -> orchestrator.status() dict (agents/cycle/goal); POST /api/cycle/start (kind in CYCLE_KINDS: research|validate|trade; 409-style error on CycleInProgressError); POST /api/cycle/stop; POST /api/goal/start; POST /api/goal/stop (GoalInProgressError); GET /api/leaderboard?limit=20 -> list[dict] (compute_leaderboard); GET /api/champions; GET /api/strategies/{strategy_id}; GET /api/backtests/{backtest_id}; GET /api/reports?limit=50 -> list[dict]; GET /api/reports/{report_id}; GET /api/logs (paged activity_log); GET /api/portfolio; GET /api/config and PUT /api/config (ConfigUpdate pydantic model; _config_view(settings) whitelists exposed keys). Module has TICKER_NAMES dict mapping universe codes to Korean display names — coinAgent equivalent: symbol -> display name map.

patterns_to_reuse: create_app(settings=None, meeting_seconds=...) factory + lifespan that builds the full object graph into app.state (settings, db, bus, loader, broker, orchestrator, ws_manager) — makes tests trivially injectable and keeps zero module-level globals except `app = create_app()`.
Startup crash-recovery sweep: UPDATE cycles SET status='aborted', finished_at=datetime('now') WHERE status='running' before anything else runs.
Lazy import of the live broker adapter inside the lifespan branch (paper mode never imports live-exchange deps/credentials code).

Auto-cycle loop that re-reads the mutable Settings instance every iteration so PUT /config changes take effect without restart; 0 = off with a 30s poll; every failure path is logged-and-continue (CycleInProgressError -> skip; generic Exception -> retry next interval) so the loop can never die silently.
Chained-cycle helper (_run_trade_cycle): retry start_cycle on CycleInProgressError with sleep, then await orchestrator.cycle_task with contextlib.suppress(Exception).
Shutdown ordering: cancel background task -> suppress(CancelledError, Exception) await it -> stop goal/cycle -> finally db.close().

Database class verbatim: single shared sqlite3 connection with check_same_thread=False + threading.Lock, WAL pragma, dict_factory rows, execute() returning list[dict] for SELECT and [{'id': lastrowid}] for writes, and additive migrations via PRAGMA table_info + ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN behind CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS.

EventBus verbatim: dataclass Event(type, agent, level, message, data); publish = persist to activity_log (UTC ISO ts, json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, default=str)) then put_nowait to every subscriber queue; payload = full row dict for type=='log', {'type': t, **data} for everything else; subscribe/unsubscribe with plain list of asyncio.Queues.

WsManager verbatim: snapshot-first ({'type':'snapshot', **orchestrator.snapshot()}), then forward-task + drain-task raced with asyncio.wait(FIRST_COMPLETED); drain loop (receive_text) is what makes client disconnects detected promptly; always unsubscribe in finally.

Settings pattern: pydantic-settings BaseSettings with SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix=..., env_file='.env', extra='ignore') + @lru_cache get_settings(); coinAgent should pick its own prefix (e.g. CA_) but keep the identical shape.
Single '/ws' endpoint delegating to state.ws_manager.handle(ws, state.bus, state.orchestrator.snapshot()); all realtime UI state flows through one discriminated-union message stream keyed by 'type'.
Serving frontend/dist via StaticFiles(html=True) mounted at '/' only when the directory exists, with CORS allowing the Vite dev origin (localhost:5173).
crypto_adaptation_notes: No market close: the auto-cycle loop pattern survives as-is (24/7 is actually simpler — no trading-calendar gating), but cadence must shift from minutes-scale research cycles to candle-close-aligned scheduling. Replace the single auto_cycle_minutes sleep with per-timeframe triggers (run analysis when a 5m/15m/4h/1d candle closes) or keep the interval loop plus a separate fast position-monitor loop (~1m/1s) for stop/TP/liquidation-distance checks that must not wait for a research cycle.

Multi-timeframe OHLCV: ohlcv_cache PK (ticker, date) must become (symbol, timeframe, ts) with timeframe in {'1d','4h','15m','5m','1m'} and ts as epoch-ms or ISO datetime (not date). Volume already REAL — fine for crypto. Add quote-volume/trades columns if the strategy layer needs them.

Settings: replace KRX universe with perp symbols (e.g. ['BTCUSDT','ETHUSDT',...]); replace initial_cash KRW int with USDT float; drop sell_tax (no Korean transaction tax) and replace commission with maker_fee/taker_fee (limit-only means maker fee ~0.02% is the normal case, but a crossing limit still pays taker — model both); add leverage: int (clamp 3-10), margin_mode: Literal['isolated'] (isolated only per requirements), funding-fee accounting toggle; replace toss_client_id/secret with exchange_api_key/exchange_api_secret (+ testnet flag); rename live_max_order_krw -> live_max_order_usdt and rethink live_daily_order_limit for 24/7 (per-hour or per-day rolling window). New env prefix (e.g. CA_) instead of SA_.

Leverage + isolated margin change the DB contracts: paper_positions needs side (long/short), leverage, isolated margin amount, entry/mark price, liquidation_price, unrealized_pnl — qty INTEGER must become REAL (fractional contracts/coins). portfolio_snapshots needs wallet_balance, margin_used, unrealized_pnl, cumulative funding paid/received, not just cash/positions_value. Add a funding_payments table (symbol, ts, funding_rate, payment) and fold funding into paper-broker mark-to-market every 8h (or per exchange schedule).

Limit-only orders break the paper broker's implicit fill model: paper_orders needs order_type='limit', limit_price, filled_qty (partial fills), status lifecycle {open, partially_filled, filled, cancelled, expired}, and a fill simulator that fills only when price trades through the limit (touch/queue assumption must be explicit); slippage setting becomes near-irrelevant for resting limits but a post-only/reprice policy (cancel-and-replace after N candles unfilled) must exist. Live adapter needs open-order tracking + cancel endpoints, which the Toss-style fire-and-forget broker interface does not have.

Split entry/exit: one decision produces N child limit orders (ladder). Add order_group/decision_id to paper_orders so entries/exits reconcile back to a single strategy decision; position sizing = per-tranche fractions of the isolated margin allocation, and exits split into partial TPs (e.g. rr-based ladder replacing single rr_ratio) plus a stop. The rr_ratio-derives-TP-from-stop convention generalizes to a list of (fraction, rr) pairs.

New event/WS types (EventBus mechanism unchanged): funding_payment, liquidation_warning, order_filled/partial_fill/order_cancelled, position_update with liq price and margin ratio — all just Event(type=..., data=...) fanned out as {'type': t, **data}; the frontend union grows but the transport contract is identical.

Risk sweep on startup must extend beyond marking cycles aborted: on boot the live broker must reconcile open positions and resting limit orders on the exchange against local DB state (crash while a position is open with 10x leverage is materially dangerous, unlike the stock paper flow); also validate leverage/margin-mode on the exchange matches settings (set isolated + leverage per symbol at startup).

Metrics/goal-seek constants tuned for daily Korean equities (min_trades_per_year=12, goal_win_rate on OOS daily bars, max_mdd=0.30) need re-baselining: with 5m/1m bars and leverage, trade counts are orders of magnitude higher and MDD on margined equity is amplified by leverage — express drawdown and returns on margin-adjusted equity including funding and fees.

WS snapshot should grow position/margin fields (open positions with liq price, margin ratio, funding countdown) since a 24/7 leveraged system's UI must show risk state immediately on connect, not only agents/cycle/meeting.

orchestrator-agents.summary: stockAgents' orchestrator + agents form the "agent office" core: a single-runner cycle state machine (Orchestrator) drives 7 persona agents (PM 준, DataEngineer 다온, Strategist 세라, Quant 민, Risk 로건, Analyst 하나, Trader 태오) through three cycle kinds — research (data → candidate generation → backtest → risk filter → champion selection → report), validate (walk-forward train/OOS split with in-memory-only backtests and a pass/fail verdict), and trade (settle pending paper orders, then place the champion's signals). Every logic hand-off is dressed as a ~4s "meeting" (meeting_start/meeting_end events) so the 2D office frontend can animate agents walking to each other; all agent activity flows through a single EventBus that atomically persists each event to the activity_log SQLite table and fans it out to WebSocket subscriber queues (each WS connection first gets a full `snapshot`, then the live stream). A goal-seek loop layered on top alternates research/validate cycles until an OOS win-rate/return/MDD/trade-count target is met (then auto-runs one trade cycle), and an auto-cycle loop kicks research on a timer. The leaderboard is a weighted percentile-rank score (sharpe .35 / win_rate .15 / mdd .2 / cagr .3) over per-ticker-averaged metrics, with low-confidence (<min_trades total) and low-activity (<min_trades_per_year annualized) strategies excluded from ranking; champion reigns are tracked in champion_history and the passed pool is auto-archived past the top 100. State is FastAPI app.state wiring: Database (thread-locked WAL SQLite, dict rows), EventBus, DataLoader, Broker (paper=two-phase pending→next-bar-open fill), Orchestrator.

key_contracts.role: Cycle state machine, meetings, goal-seek loop, leaderboard computation
key_contracts.api: CYCLE_KINDS = ('research','validate','trade'); OOS_BARS=252; MIN_VALIDATE_BARS=400; GOAL_MIN_TRADES=10; GOAL_BUSY_POLL_SECONDS=2.0. class CycleInProgressError(Exception); class GoalInProgressError(Exception). goal_met(oos: dict|None, settings) -> bool  # oos.win_rate>=goal_win_rate AND total_return>0 AND trade_count>=10 AND mdd<=max_mdd; any missing key -> False. _pct_ranks(values: list[float|None], higher_better=True) -> list[float]  # percentile ranks in [0,1], None counts as worst-1, n==1 -> [1.0]. compute_leaderboard(db, limit=20, min_trades=10, settings=None) -> list[dict]  # row = {strategy_id:int, template:str, params:dict, avg_metrics:{win_rate,sharpe,mdd,cagr,profit_factor,trade_count}, low_confidence:bool, low_activity:bool, status:'passed'|'champion'}; single JOIN query (no N+1); score = w_sharpe*rank+w_win*rank+w_mdd*rank(inverted)+w_cagr*rank; ranked first, then low-conf/low-activity rows appended unranked, sliced to limit. class Orchestrator(db: Database, bus: EventBus, settings: Settings, loader=None, broker=None, meeting_seconds=4.0, rng=None): .agents: dict[str, AgentBase] keyed pm/data/strategist/quant/risk/analyst/trader; .running -> bool (property); async start_cycle(kind='research') -> int  # raises ValueError on bad kind, CycleInProgressError if running; INSERTs cycles row (status='running', params_json={universe,candidates}), clears threading.Event _stop_flag, spawns asyncio.create_task(_run(runner, cycle_id)); async stop_cycle() -> None  # sets _stop_flag, cancels task, awaits; status() -> {trading_mode, cycle: {id,status:'running',step,kind}|None, agents:[describe()...], goal: goal_status()|None}; snapshot() -> {agents:[{id,state,detail}], cycle, meeting: {id:hex8, agents:[a,b]}|None}  # WS snapshot payload; async hold_meeting(a: str, b: str, topic: str)  # publish meeting_start{meeting_id,agents,topic} -> sleep(meeting_seconds) -> finally meeting_end{meeting_id}; .goal_running property; goal_status() -> {running,cycles_done,best_win_rate,target_win_rate,max_cycles}|None; start_goal() (sync, raises GoalInProgressError); async stop_goal(). Internal flow: async _run(runner, cycle_id)  # CancelledError -> _finalize('aborted')+log+re-raise; Exception -> _finalize('failed')+error log; finally clears meeting + sets all agents idle. async _run_research(cycle_id): pm.announce_start -> meeting(pm,strategist) -> data refresh+load -> strategist.propose(candidates_per_cycle, champion_spec, rng, elites) -> INSERT strategies rows status='candidate' -> meeting(strategist,quant) -> quant.backtest_candidates -> meeting(quant,risk) -> risk.review -> UPDATE statuses passed/rejected -> meeting(risk,strategist) -> strategist.receive_feedback([(id_key, reason)]) -> compute_leaderboard -> crown champion (demote old to 'passed', _record_champion) -> archive passed rows outside top-100 -> _evaluate_on champion for trade list -> analyst.write_report -> meeting(analyst,pm) -> _finish_cycle(summary={candidates,passed,rejected,universe,champion,report_id}) -> publish leaderboard_update{top: board[:3]} -> pm.announce_finish. Progress steps published at pct: start 0, data 5, candidates 15, backtest 25, risk 70, report 80, done 100. async _run_validate(cycle_id): split each ticker df into train=df.iloc[:-252] / oos=df.iloc[-252:], skip tickers <400 bars, need >=2; random-only propose (no champion seed — leakage); backtest_candidates(persist=False); _pick_train_champion(passed, results, spec_by_id) -> StrategySpec|None (same weighted rank); _evaluate_on(champion, test_data, cost, notional=initial_cash/len(test_data)) -> (aggregate{total_return,win_rate,mdd,sharpe,trade_count}, per_ticker list, trades list w/ amount=notional*net_ret); quant.log_trades(oos_trades); _validation_verdict -> {pass: bool, reason: str} (pass iff oos total_return>0 AND mdd<=max_mdd); analyst.write_validation_report(payload w/ train_start..test_end, tickers, skipped, champion{template,params}, train_metrics, oos_metrics, per_ticker_oos, oos_trades, verdict). Steps: start 0, data 10, train 35, oos 65, report 85, done 100. async _run_trade(cycle_id): champion_spec = _load_champion_spec() (None -> warn+finish); refresh data; trader.settle(broker); trader.execute(spec, data, broker, rr_ratio); steps start 0, settle 25, signals 60, orders 85, done 100. Helpers: _cost() -> KoreaCostModel(commission, sell_tax, slippage); _finish_cycle(cycle_id, summary) UPDATE cycles finished_at/status='done'/summary_json; _evaluate_on(spec, data, cost, notional=None) -> (aggregate, per_ticker, trades) with trade dicts {ticker,entry_date,entry_price,exit_date,exit_price,net_ret,amount|None,holding_days,open}; _record_champion(id) closes open champion_history reign + opens new (no-op if same); _backfill_champion_history(); _load_champion_spec() -> StrategySpec|None from strategies WHERE status='champion'; _load_template_elites() -> best spec per template by avg sharpe over last 2000 strategies, >=10 trades, any status; _finalize(cycle_id, status) guarded by AND status='running'; async _progress(cycle_id, step, pct) publishes cycle_progress{cycle_id,step,pct,kind}. Goal loop: while cycles_done < goal_max_cycles: research; every goal_validate_every-th run validate; track best_win_rate only among OOS-profitable runs; on goal_met break; on exhaust warn; finally _goal_auto_trade() runs one trade cycle (skipped on cancel). _goal_run_cycle(kind) retries start_cycle on CycleInProgressError every 2s, awaits task, swallows failures, returns parsed cycles.summary_json|None.

key_contracts.role: Agent base class: identity + state publication
key_contracts.api: AgentState = Literal['idle','working']. class AgentBase: class attrs id/name/role: str; __init__(self, bus: EventBus) sets state='idle', detail=''. async set_state(state: AgentState, detail: str='') -> publishes Event(type='agent_state', agent=self.id, data={'agent_id','state','detail'}). async log(message: str, level: str='info', **data) -> publishes Event(type='log', agent=self.id, level, message, data=kwargs). describe() -> {id,name,role,state,detail}.

key_contracts.role: PM agent: cycle bookends
key_contracts.api: class PM(AgentBase): id='pm', name='준', role='PM'. async announce_start(cycle_id: int)  # set_state('working', ...) + log with cycle_id kwarg. async announce_finish(cycle_id: int, summary: dict)  # log '후보 N개 중 M개 통과' + set_state('idle').

key_contracts.role: Data agent: OHLCV refresh + load via asyncio.to_thread
key_contracts.api: class DataEngineer(AgentBase): id='data', name='다온'. async refresh_universe(loader: DataLoader, tickers: list[str]) -> dict[str,int]  # to_thread(loader.refresh), logs total new rows. async load_universe(loader, tickers) -> dict[str, pd.DataFrame]  # to_thread(loader.get_ohlcv) per ticker; empty frames dropped with error log, cycle continues; set_state('idle') at end.

key_contracts.role: Strategist: evolutionary candidate generation with per-template niching
key_contracts.api: EXPLOITATION_RATIO=0.7 (champion share, no elites); CHAMPION_RATIO=0.4; ELITE_RATIO=0.3 (round-robin over elites); remainder random. class Strategist(AgentBase): async propose(n: int, champion: StrategySpec|None, rng: random.Random, elites: list[StrategySpec]|None=None) -> list[StrategySpec]  # elites filtered to exclude champion's template; champion None -> all random_candidates(n, rng); uses mutate(spec, rng) from strategies.registry. async receive_feedback(rejected: list[tuple[str, str]])  # (id_key, reason) pairs, logs first 20.

key_contracts.role: Quant: backtests in worker threads with cooperative cancellation; metric aggregation

patterns_to_reuse: Single EventBus.publish = atomic activity_log INSERT + fan-out to per-connection asyncio.Queue; WS payload shape rule: log events get the full row shape, all other types are {'type': t, **data}. Copy verbatim — it makes REST /logs and the WS stream share one source of truth.

WS handshake: send {'type':'snapshot', **orchestrator.snapshot()} immediately on accept, then relay; snapshot carries agents[{id,state,detail}] + cycle{id,status,step,kind} + meeting{id,agents} so a reconnecting client fully restores office state with no extra round trips. The _forward/_drain FIRST_COMPLETED task pair for prompt disconnect detection is also copyable.

AgentBase (id/name/role class attrs, set_state publishing agent_state events, log(**data) kwargs becoming the event data dict, describe()) — the entire office visualization keys off this tiny contract.

hold_meeting(a, b, topic): meetings at real logic hand-off points, meeting_start -> sleep(meeting_seconds) -> meeting_end in a finally block, meeting_seconds injectable so tests run fast. Choreography lives entirely in the orchestrator, never in agents.

Cycle scaffolding _run(runner, cycle_id): CancelledError -> finalize 'aborted' + re-raise; Exception -> finalize 'failed' + error log (never crash the app); finally clear meeting and force all agents idle. Plus boot-time 'UPDATE cycles SET status=aborted WHERE status=running' crash recovery.

Single-runner mutex via self.cycle_task + CycleInProgressError, and the goal/auto loops' wait-out pattern: retry start_cycle on 409 every N seconds so competing drivers interleave instead of colliding.

Cooperative cancellation of CPU-bound work: threading.Event stop_flag checked per-unit inside the worker thread + asyncio.shield around to_thread, and on CancelledError set the flag then await the shielded future before re-raising — guarantees no DB writes land after stop_cycle() returns.

Weighted percentile-rank leaderboard: _pct_ranks (None = worst) over sharpe/win_rate/mdd(inverted)/cagr with configurable weights; low_confidence and low_activity rows pass through but are excluded from ranking and appended after; the single-JOIN metrics fetch (explicitly fixed from an N+1) and the top-100 archive step that keeps the pool bounded.

Champion lifecycle: exactly one strategies row with status='champion' (old one demoted to 'passed'), champion_history reigns with demoted_at IS NULL = open reign, _record_champion no-ops when unchanged, plus the _backfill migration bridge.

Walk-forward validation kept out of production tables: persist=False backtests, throwaway enumerate() ids, random-only proposals on the train window (documented leakage rationale), verdict = OOS return>0 AND MDD<=cap; goal_met stricter than the verdict (adds win-rate + min-trade-count).

Evolutionary search with niching: champion mutations 40% + per-template elite mutations 30% round-robin + random remainder (70/30 when no elites); elites = best-per-template by avg sharpe over last 2000 strategies including rejected ones; rejection reasons fed back to strategist as (id_key, reason) pairs.

StrategySpec(template, params) + id_key() canonical string; generate_signal strips execution-only params ('stop') so signal functions stay pure; take-profit always derived as stop x rr_ratio, never an independent search parameter.

Trader idempotency: treat pending orders as executed intent (skip tickers with in-flight orders on re-run), risk exits before signal exits before entries, budget = (cash - reserved_cash) * CASH_BUFFER / n equally weighted, duck-typed broker extras (settle_pending/snapshot/reserved_cash) via hasattr so live brokers need not implement them.
Database wrapper: WAL + threading.Lock + dict_factory, execute() returning [{'id': lastrowid}] for writes, CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema + PRAGMA table_info additive column migrations.
pydantic-settings with env prefix + lru_cache get_settings, runtime-mutable via PUT /api/config setattr (loops re-read settings each iteration so config changes take effect live).
aggregate_metrics + downsample_equity(200 points, last point always kept) for cheap per-strategy drill-down storage.
crypto_adaptation_notes: Signals become 3-state: replace the {0,1} long-only signal Series with {-1,0,+1} (short/flat/long). signal_from_events needs a short-entry/short-exit variant; run_backtest, Trader.execute exits ('signal turned 0' -> 'signal flipped or turned 0'), and _today_signals int(sig>0.5) all assume long-only and must handle sign.

Position sizing with leverage + isolated margin: StrategySpec.params (or Settings) gains leverage (clamp 3-10x) and the qty math changes from int(budget // price) shares to notional = margin_budget * leverage, qty = notional / price in contract/base units (float, respect exchange stepSize/minNotional/tickSize rounding). Position dataclass needs side ('long'|'short'), leverage, isolated margin amount, and liquidation_price; Balance needs available vs locked margin. Risk agent should add a liquidation-distance check: with isolated margin, stop distance must be well inside ~1/leverage or the stop is fiction.

Limit-only orders: OrderRequest.price becomes required (no market fallback); Order gains states 'open'/'partially_filled'/'cancelled' beyond pending/filled/rejected, plus filled_qty. The paper broker's two-phase 'pending -> next bar open' fill becomes a limit-fill simulator (filled when bar trades through the limit price, maker fee); Trader needs an unfilled-order policy (cancel/reprice after N bars or a timeout) — the existing 'pending = executed intent, skip' idempotency guard must gain a max-age or it deadlocks forever on a never-filled limit.

Split entry/exit: one signal no longer maps to one order. Trader.execute should place K laddered limit entries (e.g. 3 tranches at price offsets) and split exits (partial take-profit at TP1/TP2, remainder at stop or signal flip). This means paper_orders rows need a group/intent id linking tranches to one logical position decision, and trade P/L aggregation (trades table, _evaluate_on trade dicts) must roll up partial fills.

Multi-timeframe 1d/4h/15m/5m/1m: ohlcv_cache PK becomes (symbol, timeframe, ts) and DataLoader/DataEngineer signatures gain a timeframe dimension — load_universe returns dict[symbol, dict[tf, DataFrame]]. generate_signal(spec, frames) receives the frame dict; typical layout: 1d/4h regime filter, 15m/5m entry trigger, 1m execution/fill checks. Backtest engine must iterate on the finest timeframe while aligning higher-TF context without lookahead (use only closed higher-TF bars). OOS_BARS=252 daily bars and Risk's LOOKBACK_YEARS=5.0 annualization must be re-based on bar counts per timeframe (e.g. OOS = last 30 days of 15m bars; annualize trade counts against the actual lookback window length).

Funding fees: KoreaCostModel(commission, sell_tax, slippage) -> PerpCostModel(maker_fee, taker_fee, slippage, funding). Drop sell_tax; backtester must debit/credit funding every 8h (or per exchange schedule) on open positions, sign-dependent (longs pay when rate positive). Funding-rate history becomes a new cached series per symbol; compute_metrics and trade net_ret must include accumulated funding, and the report/champion trade lines should show funding paid per trade.

24/7 market — scheduling changes: no 'next trading day open' concept. The daily research->trade cadence becomes continuous: auto_cycle_minutes stays but trade cycles should run on bar-close of the execution timeframe (e.g. every 5m/15m), with research/validate cycles interleaved via the existing single-runner mutex + wait-out pattern (reuse verbatim — it already handles competing drivers). Trader.settle's 'settle at next completed bar's open' becomes 'match open limit orders against newly closed 1m bars'. holding_days -> holding_hours/bars; all date().isoformat() truncations must keep full timestamps.

Symbols and money: universe entries become perp symbols ('BTCUSDT','ETHUSDT',...); TICKER_NAMES map and the KRW formatting in Quant.log_trades / Trader logs ('{:,.0f}원', '주') become USDT with 2-4 decimals and quantity decimals; initial_cash in USDT; live_max_order_krw -> per-order max notional USDT + max leverage + max concurrent positions guards.

New Settings keys (SA_ -> e.g. CA_ prefix): leverage_min=3, leverage_max=10, margin_mode='isolated' (read-only), maker_fee, taker_fee, funding_enabled, timeframes=['1d','4h','15m','5m','1m'], execution_timeframe, entry_tranches, exit_tranches, order_ttl_bars, exchange keys (binance/bybit api key+secret, testnet flag) replacing toss_client_id/secret. Keep goal-seek keys; goal metrics work unchanged since they read the OOS metrics dict.

Risk metrics tuning: max_mdd=0.30 is far too loose at 3-10x leverage on account equity — MDD is now on margin-adjusted equity; add per-trade risk cap (stop distance x leverage <= X% of equity) and liquidation-buffer rejection in Risk.review. rr_ratio-derived TP still works but TP/stop are now limit orders resting on the exchange, so Trader's quote-polling stop/TP checks (get_quote each cycle) should become placed reduce-only stop/TP orders, checked/replaced per cycle.

Champion cadence: with 24/7 bars and intraday timeframes, walk-forward windows shrink and regime drift is faster — validate cycles should be time-based (goal_validate_every stays, but consider auto-demoting a champion whose live rolling P/L breaches a drawdown gate; champion_history schema already supports it).

backtest-strategies.summary: The backtest+strategies subsystem of stockAgents is a small, pure, dependency-light core (647 LOC total) that the agent office (quant agent, orchestrator, trader) drives. Strategies are pure functions `params x OHLCV DataFrame -> {0,1} signal Series`; the engine (`backend/app/backtest/engine.py`) is strategy-agnostic and consumes only the signal. Execution semantics: a signal decided at bar t's close is executed at bar t+1's open via `signal.shift(1)` (structural no-lookahead, enforced by a regression test); a fill mask blocks entries on limit-up gaps (open >= prev_close*1.299) and blocks/carries exits on limit-down gaps (open <= prev_close*0.701); costs are asymmetric per-side fractions applied to fill prices (KoreaCostModel: buy = commission+slippage, sell = commission+sell_tax+slippage); returns are computed as partial-day (entry day open->close from the cost-adjusted fill, exit day prev_close->cost-adjusted open, hold days close/prev_close), then compounded to an equity curve. Engine-level forced exits (stop_pct/take_profit_pct, both optional, both referenced to the raw entry-day open) trip on a bar close and execute next open, with a re-entry block until the signal re-arms to 0. The take-profit is never independent: call sites always derive take_profit_pct = stop*rr_ratio (rr_ratio=3.0 in config.py). Metrics (metrics.py) return a plain dict with None (never NaN/inf) for undefined values, a profit-factor cap at 99.0, and a low_confidence flag under 10 trades. The strategies package provides 7 templates registered in registry.py with ParamRange grids, a shared `stop` execution param (0.03-0.10, filtered out before signal functions are called), random candidate sampling, and +/-20% clamped mutation for evolutionary parameter search used by the quant agent.

key_contracts.role: Core backtest engine: long-only, next-open execution, fill mask, stop/take-profit forced exits, partial-day return accounting, equity compounding
Asymmetric per-side trading cost model applied to fill prices
key_contracts.api: @dataclass(frozen=True) KoreaCostModel: commission: float = 0.00015 (per side); sell_tax: float = 0.0018 (sell only); slippage: float = 0.0005 (per side). @property buy_cost -> commission + slippage. @property sell_cost -> commission + sell_tax + slippage. Costs are fractions multiplied onto fill prices: buy fill = open*(1+buy_cost), sell fill = open*(1-sell_cost).

def build_trades_frame(records: list[TradeRecord]) -> pd.DataFrame.

key_contracts.role: Performance metrics with None-guards for undefined values (never NaN/inf)
key_contracts.api:
Constants: PROFIT_FACTOR_CAP = 99.0; LOW_CONFIDENCE_TRADES = 10.
def compute_metrics(result: BacktestResult, periods: int = 252) -> dict with keys: trade_count: int; win_rate: float|None ((net_ret>0).mean()); profit_factor: float|None (gross_profit/gross_loss capped at 99.0; = 99.0 if no losses but some profit; None if both zero or no trades); sharpe: float|None (daily_ret.mean()/std(ddof=1)*sqrt(periods), None if n<2 or std<=0/non-finite); mdd: float|None (positive fraction; equity prepended with 1.0 so an initial dip counts: max(1 - eq/np.maximum.accumulate(eq))); cagr: float|None (final_equity**(periods/n) - 1, None if final<=0); avg_holding_days: float|None; low_confidence: bool (trade_count < 10). `periods` is the annualization factor — 252 for daily equities.

key_contracts.role: Public backtest API surface
key_contracts.api: Exports: KoreaCostModel, BacktestResult, run_backtest, compute_metrics.

key_contracts.role: Strategy primitives: param ranges, spec identity, event->stateful-signal helper, template dispatch
Template registry with param grids, random candidate generation, +/-20% mutation with clamping, cross-param constraint repair
key_contracts.api:
STOP_RANGE = ParamRange(0.03, 0.10, is_int=False) — every template's grid includes 'stop': STOP_RANGE (stop-loss fraction; take-profit ALWAYS derived at call sites as stop*rr_ratio, never an independent param).
TEMPLATES: dict[str, ParamGrid] = { 'sma_cross': {fast: (5,20) int, slow: (20,120) int}, 'rsi_reversion': {period: (7,21), low: (20,35), high: (55,75)}, 'bollinger': {k: (1.5,3.0) float}, 'momentum_breakout': {entry_n: (20,120), exit_m: (10,60)}, 'pullback_swing': {trend_n: (40,120), rsi_n: (2,5), low: (5,25), high: (55,80)}, 'gap_momentum': {gap: (0.01,0.04) float, exit_n: (3,10)}, 'breakout_swing': {break_n: (5,15), exit_n: (3,7)} } — each plus 'stop'.
SIGNAL_FUNCS: dict[str, callable] mapping template name -> module.signal.
def _fix_constraints(template, params) -> params: for sma_cross enforce fast < slow (clamp fast to slow-1; if still violated bump slow to fast+1).
def _sample(pr: ParamRange, rng: random.Random) -> float: randint(low,high) if is_int else uniform.
def random_candidates(n: int, rng: random.Random) -> list[StrategySpec]: uniform-random template, sample every grid param, fix constraints.
def mutate(spec: StrategySpec, rng: random.Random) -> StrategySpec: each param = pr.clamp(old * rng.uniform(0.8, 1.2)); params missing from the parent (new dimensions added after the champion was born, e.g. 'stop') are sampled fresh instead of dropped; constraints re-fixed.

key_contracts.role: Template: long while fast SMA > slow SMA (stateless comparison signal)
key_contracts.api: def signal(df: pd.DataFrame, fast: float, slow: float) -> pd.Series: (close.rolling(int(fast)).mean() > close.rolling(int(slow)).mean()).astype(float).

key_contracts.role: Template: RSI mean reversion + reusable Wilder RSI helper
key_contracts.api:
def rsi(close: pd.Series, period: int) -> pd.Series: Wilder-smoothed via ewm(alpha=1/period, min_periods=period, adjust=False) on clipped gains/losses; div-by-zero-safe form 100*AG/(AG+AL).
def signal(df, period: float, low: float, high: float) -> pd.Series: entry = rsi < low, exit = rsi > high, via signal_from_events.

key_contracts.role: Template: lower-band touch entry, mid-band reversion exit
key_contracts.api: PERIOD = 20 (fixed). def signal(df, k: float): mid = close.rolling(20).mean(); lower = mid - k*close.rolling(20).std(); entry = close <= lower; exit = close >= mid; signal_from_events.

key_contracts.role: Template: Donchian-style breakout entry / breakdown exit
key_contracts.api: def signal(df, entry_n: float, exit_m: float): entry = close > high.rolling(n).max().shift(1); exit = close < low.rolling(m).min().shift(1); signal_from_events. Note the .shift(1) on channel bounds so the breakout compares against the PRIOR window (indicator-level lookahead guard, in addition to the engine's shift(1)).

key_contracts.role: Template: RSI dip inside an uptrend (2-5 day swing)
key_contracts.api: def signal(df, trend_n, rsi_n, low, high): entry = (close > close.rolling(int(trend_n)).mean()) & (rsi(close, int(rsi_n)) < low); exit = rsi > high; signal_from_events.

key_contracts.role: Template: gap-up follow-through entry, short-SMA exit
key_contracts.api: def signal(df, gap: float, exit_n: float): entry = (open >= prev_close*(1+gap)) & (close > open); exit = close < close.rolling(int(exit_n)).mean(); signal_from_events.

key_contracts.role: Template: short breakout entry, short-SMA exit
key_contracts.api: def signal(df, break_n: float, exit_n: float): entry = close > high.rolling(int(break_n)).max().shift(1); exit = close < close.rolling(int(exit_n)).mean(); signal_from_events.

key_contracts.role: Where the stop/take-profit 3:1 coupling lives (NOT in the engine)
key_contracts.api: Settings.rr_ratio: float = 3.0. All callers (orchestrator.py ~line 871, agents/quant.py ~line 165, api/routes.py ~line 133, agents/trader.py execute(..., rr_ratio=3.0)) pass take_profit_pct = spec.params.get('stop') * rr_ratio when stop is not None, else None. The engine keeps stop_pct and take_profit_pct fully independent and generic.

patterns_to_reuse: Signal purity contract: a strategy is a pure function params x OHLCV -> {0,1} float Series aligned to df.index; the engine is strategy-agnostic and consumes only the signal. Copy verbatim.

Structural no-lookahead: decide on bar t close, execute at bar t+1 open via signal.shift(1).fillna(0) inside the engine (never in strategies), and back it with a regression test. Strategies additionally .shift(1) any rolling channel bound they break out of.

signal_from_events(entry, exit_, index): NaN series -> stamp exit=0.0 first, then entry=1.0 (entry wins same-bar ties), ffill().fillna(0.0). One helper makes every event-style strategy 5 lines. Copy verbatim.

Fill mask as boolean arrays computed vectorized before the loop, consumed inside a simple stateful per-bar loop; blocked buys retry next bar while the signal stays on, blocked sells carry the position over. The state machine (pos, entry_i, entry_eff, entry_open, exit_pending, blocked) is small and testable — copy the structure.

Cost model as a frozen dataclass exposing buy_cost/sell_cost properties applied multiplicatively to fill prices (entry_eff = fill*(1+buy_cost), exit_eff = fill*(1-sell_cost)); net_ret = exit_eff/entry_eff - 1 so costs are baked into every trade and every partial-day return. Swap the fields, keep the shape.

Partial-day return accounting: entry day = close/entry_eff - 1, exit day = exit_eff/prev_close - 1, hold days = close/prev_close - 1; equity = (1+daily_ret).cumprod(). This makes the equity curve exactly consistent with the trade log.

Stop/take-profit as engine-level params referenced to the raw entry fill price, tripping on close, executing next open, with a post-forced-exit re-entry block that re-arms only when the signal returns to 0. Take-profit derived at call sites as stop * rr_ratio — never an independent search dimension.

Metrics None-guard convention: any undefined metric (no trades, zero std, empty series) is None, never NaN/inf — keeps JSON/DB serialization clean. Plus PROFIT_FACTOR_CAP=99.0 and low_confidence flag when trade_count < 10. MDD computed with a prepended 1.0 so an initial dip counts. Copy verbatim (change periods for annualization).
Canonical trade schema: TradeRecord dataclass + TRADE_COLUMNS + empty_trades_frame() with explicit dtypes + build_trades_frame(); open=True marks the end-of-data mark-to-market trade. Copy verbatim.

Registry pattern: TEMPLATES dict[str, ParamGrid] + SIGNAL_FUNCS dict[str, callable] + _fix_constraints for cross-param invariants (fast<slow) + random_candidates(n, rng) + mutate(spec, rng) with param = clamp(old * uniform(0.8, 1.2)) and fresh-sampling of params the parent predates (forward-compatible champion lineage). Copy verbatim.
StrategySpec.id_key() -> 'template(k=v,...)' with sorted keys and %g formatting as the canonical strategy identity / Series name / champion key.
Explicit rng: random.Random threaded through random_candidates and mutate for reproducible search.
'stop' as an execution param carried in the same params dict as signal params but stripped by generate_signal before dispatch — one search space, pure signals.
crypto_adaptation_notes: Cost model: replace KoreaCostModel with a CryptoCostModel — maker_fee (limit orders, e.g. 0.02% or negative rebate), taker_fee, slippage, and NO sell_tax; since coinAgent is limit-only, both sides use maker_fee. Keep the frozen-dataclass buy_cost/sell_cost property shape. Add funding_rate handling separately (see below) — funding is time-based, not per-fill, so it belongs in the per-bar return loop, not in the fill-price multiplier.

Fill mask: drop the Korean +/-30% limit-up/down mask entirely (no price limits in crypto). Replace it with a limit-order fill model: an entry limit at price P fills on bar t only if low[t] <= P (long) / high[t] >= P (short); unfilled orders either retry or expire after k bars. The engine's 'blocked buy retries next bar / blocked sell carries position' state machine maps directly onto 'limit not filled yet'.

Shorts and leverage: the engine is long-only with pos in {0,1}; perp futures need pos in {-1, 0, +1} (or signed float for split entries) and a leverage multiplier L (3-10x). With isolated margin, per-bar return = L * price_return - costs, and add a liquidation check: for longs, if low[t] <= entry_price * (1 - 1/L + maintenance_margin_rate), the position is wiped to approximately -100% of isolated margin (bankruptcy price), overriding stop logic. Liquidation must be checked on intrabar low/high, not close, unlike the close-based stop.

Stop/take-profit: keep the entry-price-referenced stop and stop*rr_ratio coupling, but express thresholds in margin terms (a 3% price stop at 5x = 15% margin loss) — the STOP_RANGE (0.03-0.10 on price) must shrink or be defined on margin PnL; also check breaches against intrabar low/high rather than close, since 24/7 bars have no overnight gap to hide behind, and execute the forced exit as a limit order with a fallback.

Split entry/exit: replace the binary {0,1} signal with either a target-fraction signal in [0,1] (or [-1,1]) consumed as a position size, or keep {0,1} signals per tranche and run N parallel tranches. The TradeRecord/trades frame then needs a size/notional column and net_ret weighted by size; signal_from_events generalizes by stamping fractional levels instead of 1.0.

Multi-timeframe (1d/4h/15m/5m/1m): the engine already takes any DatetimeIndex — run it per-timeframe, but (a) holding_days = (exit-entry).days breaks; store holding_bars and holding_hours instead; (b) metrics' periods=252 must become the per-timeframe annualization factor (365 for 1d since crypto trades every day, 6*365 for 4h, 96*365 for 15m, 288*365 for 5m, 1440*365 for 1m); (c) higher-timeframe regime filters (e.g. 1d trend gating 15m entries) need an explicit resample-then-ffill-to-lower-timeframe helper with .shift(1) at the higher timeframe to avoid lookahead across timeframes; (d) TEMPLATES param ranges are calibrated in daily bars — either scale ranges per timeframe or define per-timeframe grids.

Funding fees: perps charge funding every 8h (or 1h on some venues). Add a funding leg to the per-bar loop: while in position at a funding timestamp, daily_ret[t] -= sign(pos) * funding_rate[t] * L (longs pay when funding is positive). Requires a funding-rate series aligned to the OHLCV index; for backtests without historical funding, a constant approximation (e.g. 0.01%/8h) as a config key.

No market close / 24/7: no day-boundary semantics anywhere — 'next open' becomes simply 'next bar open' with open[t+1] == close[t] typically (no gaps), which makes gap_momentum meaningless (drop or redefine as wick/impulse momentum); annualize with 365 not 252; the orchestrator's daily cadence becomes a continuous loop keyed on bar-close events per timeframe.

Strategy templates: sma_cross/rsi_reversion/bollinger/momentum_breakout/pullback_swing/breakout_swing port directly (they only read OHLCV); add short-side mirrors (signal -> {-1,0,1}) and crypto-native templates (funding-rate mean reversion, open-interest/volume filters). Keep the registry+mutate machinery unchanged — only TEMPLATES contents and grids change; add 'leverage': ParamRange(3, 10) and possibly 'timeframe' as searchable execution params stripped by generate_signal exactly like 'stop'.

Metrics: keep the None-guard dict shape; rename avg_holding_days to avg_holding_bars/hours; add funding_paid, liquidation_count, max_leverage-adjusted MDD on margin; low_confidence threshold of 10 trades stays sensible.

The rr_ratio=3.0 coupling in config Settings and the pattern of passing take_profit_pct = stop * rr_ratio at every call site (orchestrator/quant/routes/trader) carries over verbatim — keep the engine's two thresholds generic and independent.

broker-data-reports.summary: The broker/data/reports subsystem of stockAgents is the execution and I/O layer under the "agent office" trading loop. A single abstract Broker ABC (backend/app/broker/base.py) defines four async methods (get_quote, get_balance, place_order, get_orders) over five plain dataclasses (Quote, Position, Balance, OrderRequest, Order); PaperBroker (paper.py) implements it as a two-phase simulator — place_order records a 'pending' row with the decision bar's date/close, and settle_pending() (called after the daily data refresh) fills at the NEXT completed bar's open with asymmetric costs and the Korean ±30% price-limit fill mask, persisting all state in SQLite (paper_orders/paper_positions/paper_state/portfolio_snapshots); TossBroker (toss.py) implements the same interface against the Toss Securities REST API with a lock-serialized single-token OAuth2 manager, lazy account-seq resolution, 429 exponential backoff + 401 token invalidation retry, and two local safety guards (per-order notional cap live_max_order_krw, per-day order count live_daily_order_limit) that reject orders before they ever hit the wire. DataLoader (data/loader.py) serves adjusted daily OHLCV DataFrames cache-first from the ohlcv_cache SQLite table, incrementally fetching only missing dates from FinanceDataReader with a yfinance (.KS then .KQ) fallback, always dropping the in-progress session's bar, and degrading to cache-only when all sources fail. reports/generator.py deterministically renders Korean markdown reports (cycle research report and walk-forward validation report) from plain dicts — leaderboard, champion metrics, and full trade tables — with '–' placeholders for missing values; no LLM in the render path. Everything is wired through pydantic-settings Settings (env prefix SA_) and a thread-locked WAL-mode SQLite Database wrapper whose execute() returns list[dict] for SELECTs and [{"id": lastrowid}] for writes.

key_contracts.role: Broker ABC + shared order/position dataclasses — the single contract both paper and live brokers implement; agents depend only on this
Paper broker: two-phase next-open fill simulation matching the backtest engine's execution model, state in SQLite
key_contracts.api:
class PaperBroker(Broker):
    def __init__(self, db: Database, loader: DataLoader, settings: Settings)  # builds KoreaCostModel(commission, sell_tax, slippage); _ensure_cash() seeds paper_state key 'cash' = settings.initial_cash via INSERT OR IGNORE
    async def get_quote(symbol) -> Quote            # last cached close via asyncio.to_thread; raises ValueError if no data
    async def get_balance() -> Balance              # cash from paper_state + positions WHERE qty > 0 ORDER BY ticker
    def reserved_cash() -> float                    # SUM(qty*decision_price) of pending buys * (1 + buy_cost); subtract from Balance.cash for spendable
    async def place_order(request) -> Order         # validates then records 'pending' with decision_date=quote.ts, decision_price=quote.price. Immediate rejects: qty<=0 → 'invalid qty'; quote failure → 'no quote: {exc}'; buy where qty*price*(1+buy_cost) > cash - pending_buy_reserved → 'insufficient cash'; sell where held - pending_sell_qty(ticker) < qty → 'insufficient position'; bad side → 'invalid side: {side}'
    def settle_pending() -> list[Order]             # for each pending order: df.index.searchsorted(decision_date, side='right'), scan forward; buy skips bars where open >= prev_close*LIMIT_UP_RATIO (1.299), sell skips where open <= prev_close*LIMIT_DOWN_RATIO (0.701); fill at open. Buy fill: total=qty*open*(1+buy_cost), reject if > cash ('insufficient cash at settlement'), else avg_price = weighted avg, cash -= total. Sell fill: reject if pos missing/short ('insufficient position at settlement'), proceeds=qty*open*(1-sell_cost), delete row when qty hits 0. Loader failure → stays pending, retried next cycle. Returns only orders whose status changed.
    async def get_orders(limit=50) -> list[Order]   # ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT ?
    def snapshot() -> None                          # INSERT INTO portfolio_snapshots(cash, positions_value, total_value); price falls back to avg_price if quote fails
# cost model: KoreaCostModel.buy_cost = commission + slippage; sell_cost = commission + sell_tax + slippage

key_contracts.role: Live broker adapter (Toss Securities REST): OAuth2 single-token management, backoff, account header, local safety guards
key_contracts.api:
BASE_URL = 'https://openapi.tossinvest.com'; TOKEN_REFRESH_MARGIN = 600.0; MAX_RETRIES = 2
class TossConfigError(RuntimeError)  # raised by __init__ when client_id/secret missing (live mode refuses to boot), or when account resolution fails
def _unwrap(payload) -> Any  # Toss wraps bodies in {'result': ...}
class TokenManager:
    def __init__(self, client: httpx.AsyncClient, client_id: str, client_secret: str)
    async def get_token() -> str   # POST /oauth2/token, data={'grant_type':'client_credentials'}, HTTP Basic auth; caches until monotonic deadline = now + max(expires_in - 600, 30); all issuance behind one asyncio.Lock because the provider keeps only ONE valid token per client
    def invalidate() -> None       # drop cache (after 401)
class TossBroker(Broker):
    def __init__(self, settings: Settings, *, transport: httpx.AsyncBaseTransport | None = None, base_url: str = BASE_URL, retry_base_delay: float = 0.5)  # injectable transport = the test seam
    async def aclose() -> None
    async def _request(method, url, *, with_account: bool = False, **kwargs) -> httpx.Response  # loop attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES+1): Bearer header; if with_account add 'X-Tossinvest-Account: {accountSeq}'; 429 → sleep retry_base_delay * 2**attempt and retry; 401 → invalidate token and retry; then raise_for_status()
    async def _resolve_account() -> str  # GET /api/v1/accounts once, cached behind asyncio.Lock; tries keys accountSeq/account_seq/seq
    async def get_quote(symbol)   # GET /api/v1/prices?codes={symbol}; price from price|currentPrice|close; ts from dateTime|ts|now()
    async def get_balance()       # GET /api/v1/assets with_account=True; cash from cash|deposit; positions from positions|stocks with code|ticker, quantity|qty, averagePrice|avg_price
    async def place_order(request) -> Order  # guard 1: daily counter reset when date.today() changes; >= live_daily_order_limit → local reject 'daily order limit reached (N orders/day)'. guard 2: notional = qty * (request.price or live quote); > live_max_order_krw → local reject 'order value X KRW exceeds live_max_order_krw Y'. Then POST /api/v1/orders with_account body {code, side, quantity, orderType: 'market'|'limit'[, price]}; HTTPStatusError → rejected 'order failed: HTTP {code}'; any HTTPError → rejected 'order failed: {exc}'; counter increments ONLY after a successful submit; response status in ('rejected','cancelled') maps to 'rejected', everything else 'filled'
    async def get_orders(limit=50)  # GET /api/v1/orders?size={limit} with_account; unwraps {'orders': [...]} shape
# rejected orders get synthetic id f'rejected-{uuid4.hex[:8]}'

key_contracts.role: OHLCV loader: SQLite cache-first, incremental fetch, source fallback chain, in-progress-bar exclusion
Deterministic Korean markdown report rendering from plain dicts (LLM narration deliberately NOT in the render path)
pydantic-settings config — env prefix SA_, .env support, extra='ignore', @lru_cache get_settings()

def get_settings() -> Settings  # @lru_cache singleton

key_contracts.role: Thread-safe WAL SQLite wrapper + full schema (broker/data tables inline here)
key_contracts.api:
class Database:
  def __init__(self, path: str)   # check_same_thread=False, dict row_factory, PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, init_schema()
  def execute(sql, params=()) -> list[dict]   # SELECT → rows as dicts; write → commit + [{'id': lastrowid}]
  def executemany(sql, seq_of_params) -> None
  def close() -> None
# migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS script + _add_columns() additive ALTERs (PRAGMA table_info diff)

ohlcv_cache(ticker TEXT, date TEXT, open REAL, high REAL, low REAL, close REAL, volume REAL, PRIMARY KEY(ticker, date))

key_contracts.role: Frozen dataclass cost model shared by backtest engine and PaperBroker (single execution model everywhere)
key_contracts.api:
@dataclass(frozen=True) KoreaCostModel(commission=0.00015, sell_tax=0.0018, slippage=0.0005)
  buy_cost -> commission + slippage           # applied as fill_price * (1 + buy_cost)
  sell_cost -> commission + sell_tax + slippage  # applied as fill_price * (1 - sell_cost)
# engine.py constants reused by paper.py: LIMIT_UP_RATIO = 1.299, LIMIT_DOWN_RATIO = 0.701

patterns_to_reuse: Single Broker ABC with paper/live implementations sharing identical async signatures and plain dataclasses (Quote/Position/Balance/OrderRequest/Order) — agents/orchestrator never know which mode is running; only the factory reads settings.trading_mode.

Two-phase paper execution: place_order records 'pending' with decision_date + decision_price, a separate settle_pending() fills at a later bar using exactly the backtest engine's fill rules and shared cost-model dataclass — paper trading and backtests can never disagree about execution.

Reservation accounting for pending orders: spendable cash = balance - reserved_cash() (SUM of pending-buy notionals * (1+buy_cost)); sellable qty = held - pending_sell_qty(ticker). Prevents double-spending across the pending window without any locking.

Rejected-instead-of-raised orders: every failure path (invalid qty, no quote, insufficient funds, safety-guard, HTTP error) returns Order(status='rejected', reason='...') so callers have one code path and every rejection is auditable in the orders table.

Local pre-flight safety guards on the live broker that reject BEFORE any network call: per-order notional cap (live_max_order_krw) and per-day order count (live_daily_order_limit) with a date-rollover counter, plus constructor-time key validation raising a config error so live mode cannot boot half-configured.

TokenManager: single cached token behind an asyncio.Lock with monotonic-clock early-refresh margin (expires_in - 600s, floor 30s) and invalidate() on 401 — required whenever the venue keeps only one valid token per client; also correct for exchanges with strict auth.

_request() retry loop: 429 → exponential backoff (base_delay * 2**attempt, MAX_RETRIES=2), 401 → invalidate token and retry, then raise_for_status(); injectable httpx transport + base_url + retry_base_delay in the constructor as the test seam.
_unwrap() envelope helper + defensive multi-key extraction (data.get('price', data.get('currentPrice', ...))) for loosely-specified venue payloads.

DataLoader cache-first flow: read SQLite cache → freshness check against last-complete-period → incremental fetch from cache_max+1 → normalize → exclude the in-progress bar → INSERT OR REPLACE upsert → re-read cache as the single source of truth. Plus source fallback chain ending in cache-only, and refresh() that swallows per-symbol exceptions so one bad symbol never kills a cycle.

_normalize() contract: lowercase column names, fixed [open,high,low,close,volume] float columns, normalized deduped sorted DatetimeIndex, dropna — every consumer (engine, broker, agents) assumes exactly this frame shape.

Deterministic markdown report generators that take plain dicts and format with None → '–' helper functions (_pct/_num/_price/_won); LLM narration kept out of the render path so reports are reproducible and testable with string assertions.

Database wrapper conventions: dict rows via row_factory, WAL mode, one threading.Lock, execute() returning [{'id': lastrowid}] for writes, schema as one CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS script plus additive _add_columns ALTER migrations, and paper_state as a generic key/value table for scalar broker state.
PaperBroker.snapshot() writing (cash, positions_value, total_value) rows for the equity-curve UI, with quote failure falling back to avg_price (book value).
crypto_adaptation_notes: Order/position quantities: change qty from int (KRX shares) to Decimal/float contract size with exchange stepSize/minQty/minNotional rounding; prices need tickSize rounding. Add a shared SymbolFilters contract (per-symbol precision rules fetched from exchange info) that both paper and live brokers consult.

Extend OrderRequest/Position for perps: OrderRequest gains leverage: int (3-10), margin_mode: Literal['isolated'] (isolated only), reduce_only: bool, order_type='limit' (limit-only policy — drop the price=None market path or make it a hard local reject), time_in_force (GTC/post-only), client_order_id for idempotency. Position gains side ('long'/'short'), leverage, isolated_margin, liquidation_price, unrealized_pnl, mark_price. Balance becomes margin-aware: wallet_balance, available_balance, total_margin, unrealized_pnl.

Order lifecycle changes fundamentally with limit-only orders: 'pending'/'filled'/'rejected' is not enough — add 'open', 'partially_filled', 'cancelled', 'expired' plus filled_qty and avg_fill_price; the Broker ABC needs cancel_order(order_id) and get_open_orders(), and the paper simulator must fill limit orders by scanning subsequent bars' high/low (buy fills when low <= limit price, sell when high >= limit) instead of next-open fills — the KRX ±30% limit-up/down fill mask (LIMIT_UP_RATIO/LIMIT_DOWN_RATIO) is deleted entirely, crypto has no price limits.

Split entry/exit: model a 'position plan' above the broker (N limit entry orders at laddered prices, M take-profit exits + stop) rather than one order per decision; the reservation-accounting pattern generalizes — reserved margin = SUM(open buy-side notional / leverage * (1 + fee buffer)) and reduce-only exits reserve position size, not cash.

Paper fill/PnL math for perps: replace KoreaCostModel with maker/taker fee model (e.g. maker 0.02%, taker 0.05%, no sell tax; limit-only → mostly maker); PnL = (exit - entry) * qty * direction, margin = notional / leverage; simulate isolated-margin liquidation (mark price crossing liq price wipes that position's isolated margin) and funding: every 8h (or per-exchange interval) apply funding_rate * position_notional signed by side — needs a funding_rates cache table and a funding_payments ledger table alongside paper_orders.

DataLoader goes multi-timeframe: ohlcv_cache PRIMARY KEY becomes (symbol, timeframe, ts) with timeframe in {'1d','4h','15m','5m','1m'} and ts as epoch-ms or ISO datetime (not date); get_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe, limit/lookback) replaces years=5; freshness check '_last_complete_session()' (weekday logic) becomes 'last closed candle = floor(now, timeframe_delta) - timeframe_delta' since crypto trades 24/7 with no holidays; in-progress-bar exclusion rule stays but keyed on candle close time, not calendar date. Fetch source: exchange REST klines (ccxt or native Binance futures API) as primary — keep the fallback-chain + cache-only-degradation pattern with a second venue or aggregator as fallback. 1m data volume forces retention limits/pruning per timeframe.

No market close → no natural 'daily cycle' anchor: settle_pending()/snapshot()/refresh() move from a once-daily post-close job to a continuous scheduler (per-timeframe candle-close triggers, e.g. every 5m/15m/4h); portfolio_snapshots gains cadence config; TossBroker's per-calendar-day order counter should become a rolling-24h (or UTC-day) counter and add a per-hour rate guard since the bot can trade around the clock.

Live safety guards, re-based for perps: live_max_order_krw → max_order_notional_usdt AND max_position_notional_usdt per symbol; add leverage cap enforcement (reject any request.leverage outside [3,10]), isolated-mode assertion (set margin mode + leverage via exchange endpoints on startup, reject if venue reports cross), max concurrent positions, and a daily max-loss circuit breaker keyed off snapshots. Keep the constructor-raises-without-API-keys pattern (e.g. BinanceConfigError) verbatim.

Auth/transport: exchange APIs use HMAC-signed requests (api_key header + signature + timestamp + recvWindow) instead of OAuth2 — TokenManager is replaced by a request signer, but keep the 429-backoff loop, honor Retry-After / X-MBX-USED-WEIGHT style headers, add 418 (IP ban) as fatal, and add clock-drift handling (server time sync) which has no KRX analog.

Add a user-data WebSocket for live order/position updates (listenKey keepalive every ~30min mirrors the TokenManager refresh-margin pattern) — REST polling get_orders(limit) alone is insufficient when limit orders rest on the book; WS execution events should update the same orders table the REST path writes.

Config keys to mirror with a new prefix (e.g. CA_): trading_mode paper|live, universe → list of perp symbols ['BTCUSDT','ETHUSDT',...], initial_cash → initial_margin_usdt, commission/sell_tax/slippage → maker_fee/taker_fee/slippage, plus leverage_default, leverage_min=3, leverage_max=10, margin_mode='isolated', timeframes=['1d','4h','15m','5m','1m'], funding_enabled, max_order_notional_usdt, max_position_notional_usdt, daily_order_limit, max_daily_loss_pct, api_key/api_secret (+ testnet flag — crypto has real testnets, a live-mode rehearsal tier stockAgents lacks).

Reports: replace KRW formatters (_won '+,.0f원', _price ',.0f') with USDT formatters carrying 2-4 decimals; trade rows gain side (long/short), leverage, funding_paid, fee_paid, and liquidation flag; holding_days becomes holding_hours/duration; add a funding-cost section and per-timeframe signal attribution; keep the deterministic-dict-in/markdown-out design and '–' placeholder convention unchanged.

api-tests.summary: stockAgents' API + test subsystem is the outer contract of an "agent office" auto-trading backend. A FastAPI app (create_app(settings, meeting_seconds) in app/main.py) wires: Settings (pydantic, .env), Database (thread-safe SQLite/WAL, dict rows), EventBus (persist every event to activity_log + fan-out to asyncio.Queue subscribers → /ws WebSocket), Orchestrator (7 named agents: pm/준, data/다온, strategist/세라, quant/민, risk/로건, analyst/하나, trader/태오; runs 'research' | 'validate' | 'trade' cycles with scripted 2-agent meetings), a broker (PaperBroker with two-phase next-open fills, or TossBroker live), and a goal-seek loop (research→validate until goal_met or budget, then auto trade cycle). backend/app/api/routes.py exposes the whole thing as /api/* REST: status, cycle start/stop (409 on overlap), goal start/stop, leaderboard, champions (current reign + demoted history), strategy/backtest drill-down, reports, paginated logs, portfolio, config get/put. The test suite (backend/tests) is 100% offline: conftest builds deterministic synthetic OHLCV (sine + trend + seeded noise + optional ±30% limit-locked opens); e2e tests seed it into ohlcv_cache, monkeypatch DataLoader._fetch → None, and drive full cycles through TestClient or a real uvicorn server, asserting exact REST/WS/DB shapes. Enforced invariants: no look-ahead (signal at close k executes at open k+1 at the earliest; mutating the signal from index k never changes returns ≤ k; a last-bar signal never executes), no same-bar fills (orders are 'pending' until the next completed bar's open), ±30% price-limit fill mask parity between engine and paper broker, asymmetric costs (sell tax), pending orders reserve cash/shares → same-day cycle re-runs are idempotent, stop/take-profit coupled via take_profit = stop × rr_ratio, exactly one champion with an auditable champion_history reign log, validate cycles never pollute leaderboard tables, stop_cycle joins worker threads (no orphan DB writes), and lifespan cleanup always closes the DB.

key_contracts.role: REST contract (router = APIRouter(prefix='/api')); handlers pull request.app.state.{orchestrator,db,settings,broker}
