POST /api/cycle/start body optional {kind: 'research'|'validate'|'trade'} (missing/invalid-JSON body → 'research'; unknown kind → 400) → {cycle_id:int, status:'running', kind}; CycleInProgressError → 409 'cycle already in progress'. POST /api/cycle/stop → {status:'stopped'}. POST /api/goal/start → {status:'running'} | 409 (GoalInProgressError); POST /api/goal/stop → {status:'stopped'}. GET /api/leaderboard?limit=20 → asyncio.to_thread(compute_leaderboard, db, limit, settings.min_trades, settings) → [{strategy_id, template, params, avg_metrics:{win_rate,sharpe,mdd,cagr,profit_factor,trade_count}, low_confidence, low_activity, status}]. GET /api/champions → {current: null|{strategy_id,template,params,avg_metrics,low_confidence,low_activity,status:'champion',crowned_at,stop_pct,take_profit_pct(=stop×settings.rr_ratio or null),backtests:[{id,ticker,metrics}]}, history:[{strategy_id,template,params,crowned_at,demoted_at,avg_metrics}] newest-first, demoted only}. GET /api/strategies/{id} → {strategy_id,cycle_id,template,params,universe,status,backtests:[{id,ticker,metrics,created_at}]} | 404. GET /api/backtests/{id} → {id,strategy_id,ticker,metrics,equity_curve:[[ts,value]...],trades:[{entry_date,exit_date,entry_price,exit_price,net_ret,holding_days}],created_at} | 404. GET /api/reports?limit=50 → [{id,cycle_id,kind,created_at}] DESC; GET /api/reports/{id} → {id,cycle_id,kind,markdown,created_at} | 404. GET /api/logs?agent=&limit=50&before_id= → [{id,ts,agent,level,event_type:'log',message,data}] id DESC, keyset pagination via before_id. GET /api/portfolio → {cash, positions:[{ticker,name,qty,avg_price,cur_price(quote or avg_price fallback),pnl}], snapshots: last 100 {ts,cash,positions_value,total_value} ASC}. GET/PUT /api/config → {trading_mode(read-only), universe:list[str], auto_cycle_minutes:int, auto_trade_after_research:bool, max_mdd, min_trades, min_trades_per_year, candidates_per_cycle, rank_w_sharpe, rank_w_win_rate, rank_w_mdd, rank_w_cagr}; PUT body = pydantic ConfigUpdate, all fields Optional, exclude_none setattr onto live settings (in-memory only).

key_contracts.role: SQLite wrapper + full schema (WAL, threading.Lock, dict_factory rows)
key_contracts.api: class Database(path): execute(sql, params=()) → list[dict] for SELECT, [{'id': lastrowid}] for writes (auto-commit); executemany(sql, seq); close(). init_schema() = CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + _add_columns() additive ALTER TABLE migrations (paper_orders.decision_date/decision_price; cycles.kind, reports.kind DEFAULT 'research'). Tables: ohlcv_cache(ticker,date TEXT PK pair, open,high,low,close,volume REAL); cycles(id PK, started_at, finished_at, status DEFAULT 'running' → done|failed|aborted, params_json, summary_json {candidates,passed,rejected}, kind); strategies(id, cycle_id, template, params_json, universe_json, status: candidate|passed|rejected|champion); backtests(id, strategy_id, ticker, metrics_json, equity_curve_json, created_at); trades(id, backtest_id, entry_date, exit_date, entry_price, exit_price, net_ret, holding_days); reports(id, cycle_id, markdown, created_at, kind: research|validation); activity_log(id, ts, agent, level DEFAULT 'info', event_type, message, data_json); paper_orders(id, ts, ticker, side, qty, filled_price, status: pending|filled|rejected, reason, decision_date, decision_price); paper_positions(ticker PK, qty, avg_price); portfolio_snapshots(id, ts, cash, positions_value, total_value); paper_state(key PK, value); champion_history(id, strategy_id, crowned_at, demoted_at NULL=open reign).

key_contracts.role: EventBus — single source for WS messages and activity_log persistence
key_contracts.api: @dataclass Event(type:str, agent:str|None=None, level:str='info', message:str='', data:dict={}). EventBus(db): async publish(event) — INSERT activity_log row then put_nowait payload to all subscriber queues; subscribe() → asyncio.Queue; unsubscribe(q) idempotent. Payloads: type=='log' → {type:'log', id, ts(UTC isoformat seconds), agent, level, event_type:'log', message, data}; any other type → {type, **event.data} flattened. WS message shapes (asserted in tests): snapshot (first message on /ws connect) {type:'snapshot', agents:[{id,state,detail}], cycle:null|{...}, meeting:null|{...}}; agent_state {type, agent_id, state, detail}; meeting_start {type, meeting_id, agents:[id,id], topic} / meeting_end; cycle_progress {type, step, pct, kind} (first step 'start', last 'done' pct=100); log; leaderboard_update {type, top:[...]}. Research-cycle meeting choreography (exact order): [pm,strategist],[strategist,quant],[quant,risk],[risk,strategist],[analyst,pm]; trade cycle: [[pm,trader]]; validate: [[pm,quant],[quant,risk],[analyst,pm]].

key_contracts.role: Cycle runner + leaderboard + champion history + goal-seek loop
key_contracts.api: CYCLE_KINDS = {research, validate, trade}. class CycleInProgressError; class GoalInProgressError. Orchestrator(db, bus, settings, meeting_seconds: float, rng: random.Random|None) — async start_cycle(kind='research') → cycle_id (raises CycleInProgressError; single mutex across kinds); .cycle_task awaitable; async stop_cycle() (marks cycle 'aborted', joins asyncio.to_thread backtest workers before returning — no post-stop DB writes); .running: bool; status() → dict (see /api/status); start_goal() (sync, raises GoalInProgressError) / async stop_goal() / goal_running / goal_status() → None until started, then {running, cycles_done, target_win_rate, max_cycles}; _record_champion(sid) closes open reign + opens new (no-op if unchanged); _backfill_champion_history() idempotent; _load_template_elites() → best spec per template by sharpe with trade_count≥10; _evaluate_on(spec, {ticker: df}, cost) → (agg_metrics, per_ticker, trades) with take_profit=stop×settings.rr_ratio derived at call site. compute_leaderboard(db, limit=..., min_trades=..., settings=...) → ranked rows: weighted score of sharpe/win_rate/mdd/cagr (rank_w_* settings), single batched query (no N+1), re-applies activity filter (trade_count/LOOKBACK_YEARS < min_trades_per_year → low_activity=True, appended unranked, champion-ineligible). goal_met(oos: dict|None, settings) → bool: oos.win_rate ≥ goal_win_rate (inclusive), total_return > 0 (strict), trade_count ≥ GOAL_MIN_TRADES, mdd ≤ max_mdd (inclusive); None/missing metric → False. Goal loop: research → validate every goal_validate_every cycles, stop on goal_met (logs '🎯 목표 달성') or goal_max_cycles budget ('목표 미달'), always chains one final trade cycle ('모의거래 자동 실행').

key_contracts.role: App factory + auto-cycle background loop + lifespan cleanup
key_contracts.api: create_app(settings: Settings, meeting_seconds: float = ...) → FastAPI with app.state.{orchestrator, db, settings, broker}. async _auto_cycle_loop(orchestrator, settings): every settings.auto_cycle_minutes start_cycle('research') then await orchestrator.cycle_task; if settings.auto_trade_after_research chain start_cycle('trade'); must swallow ANY start_cycle exception (RuntimeError, CycleInProgressError) and keep looping. Lifespan finally block: cancel auto task, swallow its stored exception, run stop_cycle() and db.close() unconditionally (post-shutdown db.execute raises sqlite3.ProgrammingError).

key_contracts.role: Broker interface + paper broker with two-phase next-open settlement
key_contracts.api: Dataclasses: OrderRequest(ticker, side:'buy'|'sell', qty:int, price:float|None=None — None=market); Order(id:str, ticker, side, qty, filled_price:float|None, status:'pending'|'filled'|'rejected', reason:str=''); Position(ticker, qty, avg_price); Quote(ticker, price, ts); Balance(cash: float, positions: list[Position]). PaperBroker(db, loader, settings): async get_quote(ticker) → latest cached close (ValueError 'no price data' if none); async get_balance(); async place_order(req) → validates AT PLACEMENT (qty≤0→'invalid qty'; no data→'no quote'; cash minus reserved-by-pending insufficient→'insufficient cash'; position minus pending sells insufficient→'insufficient position') else status='pending' (NEVER fills on the decision close); settle_pending() → list[Order]: fills each pending at the next completed bar's OPEN after decision_date, applying buy_cost=(commission+slippage), sell_cost=(commission+sell_tax+slippage), skipping (still pending) limit-locked opens (open ≥ prev_close×1.299 blocks buys; open ≤ prev_close×0.701 blocks sells), rejecting at settlement if gap-up cost exceeds cash ('insufficient cash at settlement'); snapshot() → portfolio_snapshots row; async get_orders(limit=50) latest-first incl. rejected; all state in DB → survives restart, incl. pending orders. initial cash = settings.initial_cash (10,000,000).

key_contracts.role: Trade-cycle executor: signals → orders with risk overlays and idempotency
key_contracts.api: Trader(bus). async execute(spec: StrategySpec, data: dict[str, DataFrame|None], broker, rr_ratio: float = ...) → list[Order]. Passes: (1) risk exits per held position via broker.get_quote — stop: price ≤ avg×(1−spec.params['stop']) → sell all + log '손절 매도'; take-profit: price ≥ avg×(1+stop×rr_ratio) → sell all + log '익절 매도'; both skipped if a sell for that ticker is already pending; (2) signal entries/exits from _today_signals(spec, data) → {ticker: 0|1}, skipping any ticker with an in-flight order (same-day re-run → returns [] with zero duplicates/rejections) and budgeting new buys from cash minus reserved. Uses hasattr guards for paper-only members (reserved_cash/settle_pending/snapshot) so live brokers work. No champion → trade cycle logs '챔피언 없음' and finishes 'done'.

key_contracts.file: backend/app/backtest/engine.py + metrics.py + costs.py (contract via test_engine.py, test_metrics.py)
key_contracts.role: Vectorized backtest engine with the enforced execution model
key_contracts.api: KoreaCostModel(commission=0.00015, sell_tax=0.0018, slippage=0.0005); .buy_cost=commission+slippage; .sell_cost=commission+sell_tax+slippage. run_backtest(df: OHLCV DataFrame, signal: pd.Series of {0.,1.} aligned to df.index, cost, stop_pct: float|None=None, take_profit_pct: float|None=None) → BacktestResult(equity: Series (start 1.0, cumprod), daily_ret: Series aligned to df.index, trades: DataFrame columns exactly [entry_date, exit_date, entry_price, exit_price, net_ret, holding_days, open]). Execution model: signal decided at close t → entry at open t+1 (entry-day return = close/(open×(1+buy_cost))−1); exit at open t+1 after signal-off close t (exit-day return = open×(1−sell_cost)/prev_close−1); ±30% limit mask: locked open blocks the fill, order/position carries to the next unlocked open; stop: close ≤ entry_open×(1−stop_pct) → forced exit next open, re-entry blocked until signal goes 0 then 1 again ('re-arm'); take-profit symmetric at entry_open×(1+tp); stop and tp independent; None disables. Open trade at end: exit_price = last close mark-to-market, open=True. compute_metrics(res, periods=252) → dict with EXACT keys {trade_count, win_rate, profit_factor, sharpe, mdd, cagr, avg_holding_days, low_confidence}; win_rate counts net_ret>0 only; profit_factor = gross_win/gross_loss capped at 99.0, None if no losses+no wins; sharpe = mean/std(ddof=1)×√periods, None if std=0 or empty; mdd = positive max drawdown fraction incl. dip from initial 1.0; cagr = final^(252/n)−1; low_confidence = trade_count<10; no trades → win_rate/profit_factor/avg_holding_days None.

key_contracts.file: backend/app/strategies/ (base.py, registry.py; contract via test_strategies.py, test_strategist_niching.py)
key_contracts.role: Strategy template registry + signal generation + evolutionary ops
key_contracts.api: StrategySpec(template: str, params: dict); .id_key() → 'template(k1=v1,k2=v2)' with sorted keys. generate_signal(spec, df) → pd.Series in {0.0,1.0}, index==df.index, never NaN, warmup=0; strips execution-only params ('stop') before calling the pure signal fn; unknown template → ValueError 'unknown strategy template'. signal_from_events(entry: bool Series, exit: bool Series, index) → held state Series; entry wins on same-bar conflict; starts flat. TEMPLATES: dict[str, dict[str, ParamRange(low, high, is_int)]] for 7 templates (sma_cross fast 5-20 < slow 20-120; rsi_reversion period 7-21, low 20-35, high 55-75; bollinger k 1.5-3.0; momentum_breakout entry_n 20-120, exit_m; pullback_swing trend_n 40-120, rsi_n 2-5, low 5-25, high 55-80; gap_momentum gap 0.01-0.04 float, exit_n 3-10; breakout_swing break_n 5-15, exit_n 3-7); every grid includes stop ∈ STOP_RANGE (0.03, 0.10) float. random_candidates(n, rng: random.Random) → n specs, all params in range, ints exact, sma fast<slow. mutate(spec, rng) → new spec, same template, clamped to ranges, preserves constraints, does NOT modify parent, fills params missing from a legacy parent by fresh sampling. Strategist(bus).propose(n, champion: StrategySpec|None, rng, elites: list[StrategySpec]) → n specs: ~40% champion mutations + ~30% mutations of per-template elites (champion's own template dropped from elites) + rest random; without elites ~70% champion mutations.

key_contracts.file: backend/app/config.py (Settings keys as used across routes/tests)
key_contracts.role: pydantic Settings loaded from .env; construct in tests with Settings(..., _env_file=None)
key_contracts.api: Keys: trading_mode ('paper'|'live', default 'paper', read-only via API), db_path, universe: list[str], auto_cycle_minutes, auto_trade_after_research: bool (default False), max_mdd, min_trades, min_trades_per_year (default 12.0; 0.0 disables activity filter), candidates_per_cycle, rank_w_sharpe/rank_w_win_rate/rank_w_mdd/rank_w_cagr, rr_ratio (default 3.0), initial_cash (10_000_000), goal_win_rate, goal_validate_every, goal_max_cycles, toss_client_id/toss_client_secret, live_max_order_krw (default 100_000), live_daily_order_limit. Risk.LOOKBACK_YEARS=5 (activity threshold = min_trades_per_year × 5 trades).

key_contracts.role: Live broker adapter pattern: OAuth2, header injection, retry, hard safety guards
key_contracts.api: TossBroker(settings, transport: httpx.MockTransport|None = None, retry_base_delay: float = ...) — transport injection makes it fully testable offline; raises TossConfigError if client id/secret missing. Token manager: POST /oauth2/token with Basic base64(id:secret) → cached Bearer, asyncio.Lock single-flight, refresh margin TOKEN_REFRESH_MARGIN=600s before expiry. Account seq resolved once from GET /api/v1/accounts, sent as X-Tossinvest-Account header. place_order maps OrderRequest→{code, side, quantity, orderType:'market'|'limit', price?}; local rejects before any HTTP: qty≤0 'invalid qty'; est. notional (price or quote) > live_max_order_krw; per-day counter ≥ live_daily_order_limit (resets on date change). 429 → exponential backoff, MAX_RETRIES=2 retries; exhausted → rejected Order with '429' in reason for orders, raises httpx.HTTPStatusError for quotes. async aclose().

key_contracts.role: Shared offline fixtures — the synthetic-data foundation
key_contracts.api: make_synthetic_ohlcv(n=300, seed=42, start='2023-01-02', limit_up_at: int|None=150, limit_down_at: int|None=200) → OHLCV DataFrame on pd.bdate_range: close = 10_000 + 40t + 1500·sin(t/12) + N(0,60) seeded; open[i]=close[i−1]×(1+N(0,0.004)); optional locked opens at prev_close×1.30 / ×0.70; high/low envelope ±~0.3%. Fixtures: synthetic_ohlcv, plain_ohlcv (no locked days), cost (KoreaCostModel defaults), zero_cost, db (tmp Database, closed on teardown), settings (tmp db_path, _env_file=None). tests/test_orchestrator_e2e.py exposes seed_ohlcv(db, ticker, df): INSERT OR REPLACE into ohlcv_cache with idx.date().isoformat() — reused by test_api/test_e2e_live_server/test_goal_seek.

key_contracts.role: Offline full-stack e2e: seeded cache + disabled network + real app
key_contracts.api: Pattern: monkeypatch.setattr(DataLoader, '_fetch', lambda self, ticker, start: None) → loader serves cache only; seed 2 fake tickers ('111111','222222', 300 bars, seeds 11+i/21+i, no locked days); Settings(candidates_per_cycle=8, min_trades=3, min_trades_per_year=0.0, max_mdd=0.95); create_app(settings, meeting_seconds=0.01|0.2) then TestClient (in-proc, incl. websocket_connect('/ws')) or real uvicorn.Server on 127.0.0.1:8765 with async httpx + websockets client. Completion detection = poll GET /api/status until status['cycle'] is None (helper run_cycle_to_completion). pytest.ini: asyncio_mode=auto (bare async tests). Assertions use set(row)=={exact keys} for every payload — the shapes ARE the contract.

key_contracts.role: Cache-first OHLCV loader with tiered fallback
key_contracts.api: DataLoader(db): get_ohlcv(ticker, years=5) → normalized DataFrame cols exactly [open,high,low,close,volume], float dtypes, monotonic DatetimeIndex. Tiering: fresh cache → skip network; else incremental fetch from last_cached_date+1 (fdr.DataReader primary; yfinance {ticker}.KS then .KQ fallback); all sources down → serve stale cache; nothing anywhere → empty DataFrame. CRITICAL: the current session's (today's) still-forming bar is DROPPED (_today() hook monkeypatched in tests) — only completed bars are ever visible. refresh(tickers) → {ticker: n_new_rows}, survives per-ticker failure (0 for failed). _fetch(ticker, start) is the single network seam monkeypatched for offline mode.

patterns_to_reuse: Single network seam: all market data flows through DataLoader.get_ohlcv backed by an ohlcv_cache table, with one private _fetch method as the only network call — tests monkeypatch that one seam to None and seed the cache, making every e2e fully offline and deterministic.

Deterministic synthetic OHLCV factory in conftest (sine + trend + seeded rng noise + injectable pathological bars) exported as a plain function so any test module can build variants (more bars for walk-forward, extra settlement bar, per-ticker seeds).
Exact-shape assertions everywhere: assert set(payload) == {…exact keys…} on every REST/WS/DB row — the test suite IS the API spec; copy this discipline verbatim.

EventBus dual-write: every Event is persisted as an activity_log row AND broadcast to subscriber asyncio.Queues; WS handler = subscribe + snapshot-first message; 'log' events flatten to the activity_log row shape, all others flatten to {type, **data}. /api/logs is just a filtered view of the same table → one source of truth for UI timeline and audit.
Orchestrator single-runner mutex across all cycle kinds with typed CycleInProgressError/GoalInProgressError → HTTP 409; status() returning {cycle: null|…} doubles as the test-side completion poll.

Two-phase order model: place_order → 'pending' at decision close, settle_pending() fills at next completed bar's open with cost model + fill mask — same execution semantics as the backtest engine (engine/broker parity tests). Pending orders reserve cash/shares so same-day cycle re-runs are naturally idempotent.

Look-ahead regression test technique: run baseline backtest, corrupt the signal from index k with random values for several k, assert daily returns ≤ k are bit-identical; plus the 'last-bar signal never executes' test. Port both verbatim to every timeframe.
Champion reign ledger (champion_history with crowned_at/demoted_at NULL=open) + _record_champion no-op/close/open semantics + idempotent backfill migration.
Additive SQLite migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS master schema + _add_columns() PRAGMA table_info diff → ALTER TABLE, so old DBs upgrade in place.

App factory create_app(settings, meeting_seconds) with injected speed knob (0.0–0.2s meetings in tests) and app.state.{orchestrator,db,settings,broker}; lifespan finally-block that cancels the auto loop, swallows its stored exception, and always stop_cycle()+db.close().

Auto-cycle background loop that survives ANY start_cycle exception (log + retry next interval) with an optional chain-trade-after-research flag; tested with tiny duck-typed Recording/Flaky orchestrators.
Goal-seek loop contract: research → validate every N, goal_met(oos, settings) pure function with inclusive/exclusive boundary tests, budget exhaustion, and an always-chained final trade cycle.

Live-broker adapter testing via httpx.MockTransport injection (stateful fake API class counting requests) — token single-flight, header injection, 429 backoff, and pre-flight safety guards (max notional per order, daily order count) all tested with zero network.

Evolutionary search with per-template niching: ~40% champion mutations + ~30% per-template elite mutations (best sharpe, ≥10 trades, champion template excluded) + random rest; mutate() clamps ranges, preserves constraints (fast<slow), fills missing params for legacy lineages.

Risk exits as trader-level overlay in live/paper trading mirroring engine-level stop/take-profit in backtests, with take_profit derived as stop × rr_ratio at exactly one call site each; duplicate suppression via 'sell already pending' check.

compute_leaderboard as a pure sync function (db, limit, min_trades, settings) run via asyncio.to_thread from the route; batched single-query metrics aggregation with an N+1 regression test; re-applies eligibility filters at read time so grandfathered rows can't stay champion-eligible.
crypto_adaptation_notes: Order model: OrderRequest grows {price: float (required — limit-only), leverage: int 3–10, margin_mode: 'isolated', reduce_only: bool, position_side: 'long'|'short', time_in_force: 'GTC'|'postOnly'}; Order gains fill semantics for resting limits (partial fills, unfilled-cancel policy). The two-phase 'pending → settle at next completed bar' pattern maps naturally to 'resting limit → filled when a later bar's low/high crosses the limit price' — replace the ±30% Korean price-limit fill mask with limit-price-crossed logic (buy fills iff bar.low ≤ limit; sell iff bar.high ≥ limit) plus optional maker-only rejection when the bar opens through the price.

Positions: paper_positions needs side (long/short), leverage, isolated margin, entry/mark price, liquidation price; Balance needs {wallet_balance, available (free of isolated margin), unrealized_pnl, margin_used}. PaperBroker must simulate liquidation: if a bar's extreme crosses liq price (≈ entry × (1 ∓ 1/leverage adjusted for maintenance margin + fees)), force-close at liq price and log it. Backtest engine equally needs leverage-aware equity: daily_ret × leverage on margin, with intra-bar liquidation check preceding stop/TP checks.

Split entry/exit replaces all-in/all-out: signals become sized targets (e.g. N ladder entries at limit offsets, scaled take-profits at stop×rr multiples). trades table rows need side, leverage, and funding_paid; the trader's per-ticker 'skip if order pending' idempotency must become per-(symbol, side, price-level) since several limit orders per symbol coexist.

Costs: replace KoreaCostModel(commission, sell_tax, slippage) with PerpCostModel(maker_fee≈0.0002, taker_fee≈0.00055, slippage) — symmetric, no sell tax; limit-only strategy mostly pays maker. Add funding: every 8h (or 1h on some venues), position pays/receives funding_rate × notional; backtest engine applies it on bars crossing funding timestamps; PaperBroker applies on settle; funding history cached like OHLCV. Metrics gain funding-adjusted return; a 'costs strictly reduce performance' test still holds.

Multi-timeframe 1d/4h/15m/5m/1m: ohlcv_cache key becomes (symbol, timeframe, ts INTEGER epoch) — sub-daily needs epoch/ISO-datetime, not date strings. DataLoader.get_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe, limit) with per-timeframe cache freshness; the 'drop the still-forming current bar' rule generalizes exactly: drop any bar whose close time > now, per timeframe (the single most important invariant to keep). Signals become MTF: e.g. 1d/4h regime filter gating 15m/5m entries and 1m execution timing; generate_signal(spec, {tf: df}) and the look-ahead test must corrupt each timeframe's tail independently. Resample coarse frames from 1m in tests to guarantee cross-timeframe consistency.

No market close: 'same-day idempotency' and 'daily order limit' become rolling-window (e.g. per-hour cycle dedup keyed on bar timestamp, orders-per-24h counter with monotonic window instead of date-change reset); annualization constants change (periods=252 → 365 for daily, 365×24×4 for 15m Sharpe, etc. — make compute_metrics(periods=...) mandatory per timeframe); avg_holding_days → holding duration in hours/bars; bdate_range → fixed-frequency date_range everywhere in synthetic data; pd.offsets.BDay next-bar helpers → timedelta of the timeframe.

Exchange adapter (Binance/Bybit-style) replaces TossBroker but keeps the whole pattern: transport-injected httpx client, HMAC/API-key auth instead of OAuth2 (keep the single-flight token/listenKey manager for user-data streams), X-account header → recvWindow/timestamp signing, 429/418 backoff with Retry-After, and hard safety guards renamed live_max_order_usd + live_max_leverage + rolling order-count limit + a reduce_only-only kill-switch mode.

Universe/tickers: TICKER_NAMES dict → symbols like 'BTCUSDT' (name == symbol, drop the Korean name map or make it a config map); qty becomes float (contract/coin quantities, step-size rounding via exchange filters: tickSize/stepSize/minNotional validation at placement — new rejection reasons to test).

Auto-cycle loop runs truly 24/7 (no session guard needed — simpler than stocks) but should align research cycles to timeframe boundaries and run settle/funding sweeps on a fast timer; portfolio snapshots on an interval, not per trade cycle.

Risk model: max_mdd-style gates should be evaluated on leveraged equity; add liquidation-count==0 as a hard risk-review criterion; goal_met gains funding-inclusive total_return; stop ranges (0.03–0.10) must shrink per leverage (stop × leverage < 1/leverage margin buffer) — encode as a registry constraint like fast<slow with a clamp test.

Korean-log-string assertions ('손절 매도', '챔피언 없음', report headers) are load-bearing test contracts — decide the coinAgent log/report vocabulary up front (e.g. add '청산' for liquidation, '펀딩' for funding) and keep asserting on exact substrings.

WS/event layer, REST shapes, EventBus, leaderboard, champion_history, goal-seek, migrations, and the offline seeded-cache e2e harness need no structural change — only new fields (side, leverage, timeframe, funding) threaded through the same exact-shape assertions.

frontend.summary: stockAgents' frontend (/Users/jooyoung/joo/stockAgents/frontend) is a React 19 + Vite 8 + TypeScript SPA with zero chart/canvas/markdown libraries. It renders a pixel-art "agent office" of 7 Korean-named agents (pm/data/strategist/quant/risk/analyst/trader) on a single 2D <canvas> driven by a framework-free engine (rAF loop + per-character FSM + Manhattan waypoint routing on a 20x14 tile grid, 16px tiles at 3x scale), fed exclusively by WebSocket events from /ws. App.tsx owns all top-level state: it fans every WsEvent to (1) the OfficeEngine (visual), (2) a pure teamState reducer (팀 tab cards), and (3) switch-cased panel state (cycle info, progress, live logs, leaderboard version counter). Six tabs below the canvas: 팀(TeamPanel), 로그(LogPanel: live WS stream merged with REST-paginated history), 리더보드(Leaderboard -> BacktestDetail drill-down), 챔피언(ChampionPanel: current champion card + reign history), 리포트(ReportView with hand-rolled markdown renderer), 포트폴리오(PortfolioPanel with hand-rolled SVG EquityChart). A REST client (lib/api.ts, same-origin, Vite dev-proxied to :8000) covers status/cycle-start-stop/goal-seek/leaderboard/champions/strategies/backtests/reports/logs/portfolio/config. lib/ws.ts reconnects with exponential backoff (500ms doubling, cap 10s); the server pushes a full `snapshot` event on every (re)connect so visual state self-heals, and App clears liveLogs + remounts LogPanel via a key bump on reconnect to avoid log-id gaps. ?demo=1 replays a scripted 63s event loop with no backend. Backend persistence is a single SQLite file (WAL, thread-locked, dict rows, CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + additive ALTER migrations) with 12 tables. Everything simulation/geometry/format-related is a pure function or DOM-free class, unit-tested with vitest in a plain node environment (no jsdom).

key_contracts.api: interface AgentMeta{id:string,name:string,color:string,role?:string}. interface CharDebug{state:CharState,pendingReturn:'idle'|'working',detail:string,pos:Point,hasPath:boolean}. class OfficeEngine{constructor(canvas:HTMLCanvasElement,agents:AgentMeta[]) — DOM-free, deskIndex=i%SEATS.length; start():void (idempotent; sizes canvas WORLD_W*SCALE x WORLD_H*SCALE, bakes background+sprites once, starts rAF); destroy():void (idempotent); dispatch(e:WsEvent):void — consumes only snapshot/agent_state/meeting_start/meeting_end, idempotent on replays; snapshot ends meetings not present and (re)starts the live one; step(dtMs:number):void public headless sim tick, dt clamped to 50ms, WALK_SPEED=56 unscaled px/s; debug(agentId):CharDebug|null; meetingCount():number}. Meeting choreography: agents[0] takes MEETING_SEATS[0] (above table, faces down), agents[1] seat 1 (faces up). Render order: background blit -> monitor glow (pulse 0.35+0.2*sin(clock*5+deskIndex)) -> y-sorted characters (walk frame alternates every 0.14s; idle bob) -> device-res UI pass: name/role labels, state icons ('z' when IDLE, blinking spark when WORKING), working speech bubble (fsm.detail truncated to 26 chars), meeting bubble alternating speaker every 1.4s with animated dots.

key_contracts.role: Pure per-character FSM (fully unit-testable transition table)
key_contracts.api: type CharState = 'IDLE'|'WORKING'|'WALK_TO_MEETING'|'MEETING'|'WALK_BACK'. type FsmEvent = {type:'agent_state',state:'idle'|'working',detail?:string}|{type:'meeting_start',meetingId:MeetingId}|{type:'meeting_end',meetingId:MeetingId}|{type:'arrived'}. class CharacterFsm{state:CharState='IDLE'; pendingReturn:'idle'|'working'='idle'; detail=''; meetingId:MeetingId|null=null; send(ev:FsmEvent):void}. Rules: agent_state while at desk flips IDLE<->WORKING; while walking/meeting it only updates pendingReturn (resumed on return); meeting_start from IDLE/WORKING snapshots pendingReturn then -> WALK_TO_MEETING (dup start for same meeting = no-op; retarget keeps pendingReturn); meeting_end only if meetingId matches -> WALK_BACK (works even if still WALK_TO_MEETING — turn around mid-walk); arrived: WALK_TO_MEETING->MEETING, WALK_BACK->pendingReturn state, else ignored.

DESKS:Point[7] (two rows: y=2 x∈{1,4,7,10}; y=6 x∈{1,4,7}); SEATS = desk+{y+1}; MEETING_TABLE={x:16,y:3,w:2,h:3}; MEETING_SEATS:[{16,2},{16,6}]. toPx(tile):Point = tile*16+8 (tile-center anchor). routeToSeat(posPx,seat):Point[] and routeToMeetingSeat(posPx,meetingSeat):Point[] — dedupe'd axis-aligned waypoint lists, first element = current pos, always routed via corridor y=10 (projectToCorridor classifies on-network positions with EPS=0.51 tolerance). interface StepResult{pos:Point,idx:number,arrived:boolean}. stepAlongPath(pos,path,idx,dist):StepResult — idx starts at 1, overshoot carries into next segment, final waypoint hit exactly.

Migration pattern: executescript(CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ...) then _add_columns via PRAGMA table_info + ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN.

patterns_to_reuse: Intent-vs-position split: WS events only set FSM intent; the rAF loop owns all positions, so characters never teleport and duplicate/replayed events are harmless. Copy engine.ts/fsm.ts verbatim — nothing in them is stock-specific.

Headless-testable engine: constructor/dispatch()/step() never touch the DOM; canvas work (sprite baking, background render, rAF) only happens in start(). Tests construct with `{} as HTMLCanvasElement` and drive step(50) in a loop — enables vitest environment:'node' with zero jsdom.

Snapshot-on-connect self-healing: server pushes a full `snapshot` WS event after every (re)connect; engine.dispatch('snapshot') ends stale meetings and reapplies all agent states, so no client-side event replay is ever needed for visuals.

Reconnect log-gap fix: onReconnect callback fires only on re-opens; App clears the live-log ring buffer and bumps a wsGeneration counter used as LogPanel's React key, forcing a remount + refetch so id-based pagination stays contiguous.

Version-counter refetch: leaderboard_update WS event just increments lbVersion; Leaderboard and ChampionPanel take {version:number} props and refetch in useEffect([version]) — decouples push notifications from fetch logic.
Single fan-out handler: one handleEvent(e:WsEvent) feeds engine.dispatch, a pure reducer (useReducer(reduceTeamEvent)), and a switch for panel state — every consumer is independently testable.
Pure reducer returning the same reference for no-op events (teamState.ts) to skip re-renders.
Id-keyed Map merge of REST history pages + live WS logs, sorted desc, paginated by before_id with PAGE_SIZE=80 and exhausted = page<PAGE_SIZE.
ApiError{status,detail} + 409-specific user notices ('already running') on cycle/goal start; transient notices auto-dismissed after 4s.
Equity-curve normalization at the API boundary: accept both [date,value] tuples and {date,value} objects (normalizeEquityCurve) so backend serialization can stay compact.
Progress tolerance: accept pct as 0..1 or 0..100, clamp, and only trust progress when progress.cycle_id === cycle.id.
10s /api/status polling as a WS-gap safety net for mode badge + cycle state.
?demo=1 scripted replay: [atMs,WsEvent][] timeline looped on a timer with synthetic log entries — full office demo with zero backend; also doubles as living documentation of the event protocol.

Programmatic art everywhere: tilemap background rendered once to an offscreen canvas and blitted per frame; 12x16 char-matrix sprites palette-swapped per agent (deterministic hair from id hash, shirt from AgentMeta.color); walkFrames() derives walk animation from the stand matrix. No image assets to manage.
Manhattan waypoint network with projectToCorridor classification + stepAlongPath overshoot-carrying stepper — dead simple, fully unit-tested routing with no pathfinding library.

Hand-rolled SVG chart with pure buildChartGeometry(data,w,h,pad) + niceTicks + formatCompact — no chart dependency; and a hand-rolled markdown-subset renderer matched to what the backend generator emits.
SQLite localStamp convention: datetime('now') is UTC without suffix; frontend appends 'Z' before parsing and renders local time. Keep this helper.
SQLite migration pattern: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS script + additive _add_columns(PRAGMA table_info -> ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN).
Vite dev proxy {'/api': 'http://localhost:8000', '/ws': {target, ws:true}} + same-origin fetch + defaultWsUrl() from location — works identically in dev and prod.
StrictMode-safe canvas wrapper: effect builds a fresh engine each run, cleanup destroys it; callback held in a ref so identity changes don't rebuild.
crypto_adaptation_notes: No market close / 24-7: stockAgents assumes daily-bar cadence (holding_days, avg_holding_days, decision_date, CAGR from daily equity). coinAgent needs timestamped (ISO datetime, not date) everywhere: TradeRow.entry_date/exit_date become entry_ts/exit_ts, holding_days becomes holding_hours (or minutes), equity curve points carry full timestamps, and shortDate()/formatTs() must render date+time. auto_cycle_minutes-style continuous scheduling becomes the norm rather than an option; the '모의거래 실행' trade cycle should run on an always-on loop keyed to candle closes per timeframe.

Multi-timeframe (1d/4h/15m/5m/1m): ohlcv_cache PK (ticker,date) must become (symbol,timeframe,open_time); add timeframe to backtests (or make a backtest row per (symbol,timeframe) pair — BacktestSummary already keys by ticker, extend to {symbol,timeframe} and reuse the ticker-chip drill-down UI as symbol+TF chips). Strategy params gain a timeframe field; CycleProgressEvent.step strings should mention the timeframe being processed. Candidate: keep 1d/4h for regime filters and 15m/5m/1m for entries — meaning the strategist/quant agents produce multi-leg strategy definitions, so strategies.params_json grows nested structure (params:Record<string,number> in the UI must loosen to Record<string,number|string> or a nested object; fmtParams needs updating).

Leverage 3-10x + isolated margin: PortfolioPosition needs side ('long'|'short'), leverage, margin (isolated collateral), liq_price, mark_price, unrealized_pnl and notional; the 4 summary tiles become 총 자산(=wallet balance) / 사용 가능 / 포지션 마진 / 미실현 손익, plus per-position liquidation-distance display. paper_positions table: ticker PK -> (symbol PK, side, qty REAL not INTEGER (fractional contracts), avg_entry, leverage, isolated_margin, liq_price). AvgMetrics stays but win_rate/mdd must be computed on margin-adjusted returns; add liquidation count as a risk metric surfaced as a badge like low_activity.

Limit-only orders + split entry/exit: paper_orders gains order_type='limit', limit_price, filled_qty, remaining_qty, status ∈ {open, partial, filled, cancelled, expired}, reduce_only flag, and a group/parent id linking the legs of a split entry or scaled exit (e.g. entry_group_id, leg_index/leg_count). The trader agent's WS detail strings and the champion card need to express ladder plans ('3분할 진입 2/3 체결'). TradeRow becomes an aggregate over legs: entry_price -> avg entry over filled legs; add fees. The stopTakeProfitLabel pattern (stop_pct/take_profit_pct + rr ratio) survives but must be leverage-aware (price-move % vs margin-return %) — label both.

Funding fees: new funding_payments table (id, ts, symbol, rate, payment, position_side) and a funding component in backtest metrics (metrics_json: funding_paid, net_ret already net of fees+funding). PortfolioResponse should include next_funding_ts and cumulative funding; the risk agent's filter set (MDD/min_trades/activity) gains a funding-drag check. TradeRow.net_ret must be net of taker/maker fees + funding accrued during the hold.

AppConfig for crypto: {trading_mode, universe:string[] (e.g. ['BTCUSDT','ETHUSDT']), timeframes:string[], leverage_min:3, leverage_max:10, margin_mode:'isolated' (read-only), order_type:'limit' (read-only), entry_splits:number, exit_splits:number, max_mdd, min_trades, funding_aware:boolean}. Keep trading_mode server-read-only like stockAgents.

Agent roster maps cleanly: pm/data/strategist/quant/risk/analyst/trader all keep their roles; ROLE_DESCRIPTIONS text changes (data: exchange OHLCV + funding-rate collection via ccxt/websocket; risk: liquidation-distance + leverage caps + funding drag; trader: limit-order ladder placement/cancel-replace). The office canvas, FSM, waypoints, sprites need zero changes beyond names/colors.

Cycle kinds likely survive as research/validate/trade, but 'trade' is no longer a one-shot market-close batch — it is a persistent execution loop; the ControlBar active/stop model still works if 'trade' toggles the live executor, but consider a fourth state or making the trade cycle long-running with cycle_progress heartbeats. GoalStatus (win-rate goal seek) ports unchanged.

Data volume: 1m candles are ~1440 rows/day/symbol vs 1 row/day — ohlcv_cache in SQLite still works but needs (symbol,timeframe,open_time) index and retention/downsampling; equity curves must be downsampled server-side like stockAgents' downsample_equity (keep the [ts,value] tuple wire format + normalizeEquityCurve boundary).

WS event volume: with 1m-timeframe trading the log/agent_state event rate rises sharply; keep MAX_LIVE_LOGS=500 ring buffer and the id-gap remount pattern — they were designed for exactly this; consider server-side throttling of cycle_progress to ≥1s intervals.
